  743: func TestPartialCandidateRequiresSucceededPreviousBatch(t *testing.T) {
  744: 	partial := rec("partial-active", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 40, 60, 1)
  745: 	fl := newFakeLedger([]domain.UsageRecord{partial})
  746: 	candidates, err := fl.LoadChargeCandidates(t.Context(), "u", "RUB")
  747: 	if err != nil {
  748: 		t.Fatal(err)
  749: 	}
  750: 	if len(candidates) != 0 {
  751: 		t.Fatalf("active partial candidate returned: %+v", candidates)
  752: 	}
  753: 	fl.seedSucceededBatchForRecord(partial)
  754: 	candidates, err = fl.LoadChargeCandidates(t.Context(), "u", "RUB")
  755: 	if err != nil {
  756: 		t.Fatal(err)
  757: 	}
  758: 	if len(candidates) != 1 || candidates[0].LocalRequestID != partial.LocalRequestID {
  759: 		t.Fatalf("candidates=%+v", candidates)
  760: 	}
  761: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{partial}, 60, time.Unix(2, 0).UTC())
  762: 	if err != nil {
  763: 		t.Fatal(err)
  764: 	}
  765: 	flNoSucceeded := newFakeLedger([]domain.UsageRecord{partial})
  766: 	if _, err := flNoSucceeded.PrepareChargeBatch(t.Context(), plan); err == nil {
  767: 		t.Fatal("expected active partial claim rejection")
  768: 	}
  769: }

  771: func TestApplyChargeSuccessUsesPersistedImmutableAllocations(t *testing.T) {
  772: 	r := rec("persisted-allocation", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  773: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{r}, 100, time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
  774: 	if err != nil {
  775: 		t.Fatal(err)
  776: 	}

  778: 	snapshot, err := fl.PrepareChargeBatch(t.Context(), plan)
  779: 	if err != nil {
  780: 		t.Fatal(err)
  781: 	}
  782: 	mutatedAllocations := append([]domain.BillingChargeAllocation(nil), snapshot.Allocations...)
  783: 	mutatedAllocations[0].ChargedAmountCents = 99
  784: 	mutatedExpected := append([]domain.UsageRecord(nil), snapshot.ExpectedRecords...)
  785: 	mutatedExpected[0].RemainingAmountCents = 99
  786: 	err = fl.ApplyChargeSuccess(t.Context(), ports.UsageChargeSuccess{BatchID: snapshot.Batch.ID, ChargedAt: time.Unix(2, 0).UTC(), Allocations: mutatedAllocations, ExpectedRecords: mutatedExpected})
  787: 	if err == nil {
  788: 		t.Fatal("expected success command mismatch")
  789: 	}
  790: }

  820: func TestOpenSucceededBatchIsStoreContractViolation(t *testing.T) {
  821: 	r := rec("open-succeeded", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  822: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{r}, 100, time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
  823: 	if err != nil {
  824: 		t.Fatal(err)
  825: 	}
  826: 	now := time.Unix(2, 0).UTC()
  827: 	snapshot := ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{Batch: plan.Batch, Allocations: plan.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: claimedExpected(plan)}
  828: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  829: 	snapshot.Batch.ChargedAt = &now
  830: 	fl := newFakeLedger(nil)
  831: 	fl.open = []ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{snapshot}
  832: 	svc, _ := NewAutoChargeService(&fakeIdentity{token: "jwt"}, &fakeBalance{balance: ports.BillingBalance{Currency: "RUB", BalanceCents: 100}}, &fakeCharge{}, fl, testClock{now}, AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 1})
  833: 	_, err = svc.Run(t.Context(), AutoChargeInput{UserID: "u", BillingSubjectUserID: "billing"})
  834: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation) {
  835: 		t.Fatalf("err=%v", err)
  836: 	}
  837: 	if fl.prepareCalls != 0 {
  838: 		t.Fatalf("prepare called after contract violation")
  839: 	}
  840: }

  842: func TestSucceededRetryClearsFailedMetadata(t *testing.T) {
  843: 	r := rec("retry-clear", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  844: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{r}, 100, time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
  845: 	if err != nil {
  846: 		t.Fatal(err)
  847: 	}
  848: 	failedAt := time.Unix(2, 0).UTC()
  849: 	snapshot := ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{Batch: plan.Batch, Allocations: plan.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: claimedExpected(plan)}
  850: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed
  851: 	snapshot.Batch.FailedAt = &failedAt
  852: 	snapshot.Batch.BillingErrorCode = "billing_unavailable"
  853: 	fl := newFakeLedger([]domain.UsageRecord{snapshot.ExpectedRecords[0]})
  854: 	fl.open = []ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{snapshot}
  855: 	chargedAt := time.Unix(3, 0).UTC()
  856: 	err = fl.ApplyChargeSuccess(t.Context(), ports.UsageChargeSuccess{BatchID: snapshot.Batch.ID, ChargedAt: chargedAt, Allocations: snapshot.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: snapshot.ExpectedRecords})
  857: 	if err != nil {
  858: 		t.Fatal(err)
  859: 	}
  860: 	succeeded := fl.batches[snapshot.Batch.ID].Batch
  861: 	if succeeded.FailedAt != nil || succeeded.BillingErrorCode != "" || succeeded.ChargedAt == nil {
  862: 		t.Fatalf("failed metadata not cleared: %+v", succeeded)
  863: 	}
  864: 	if err := ledger.ValidateChargeBatch(succeeded); err != nil {
  865: 		t.Fatalf("invalid succeeded batch: %v", err)
  866: 	}
  867: }

  896: 		t.Fatalf("err=%v", err)
  897: 	}
  898: 	if fl.applyCalls != 0 {
  899: 		t.Fatalf("apply called %d times", fl.applyCalls)
  900: 	}
  901: }
  902: 
  903: func TestRunNormalizesStoreValidationErrors(t *testing.T) {
  904: 	badCandidate := rec("store-bad-model", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 10, 0, 10, 1)
  905: 	badCandidate.BillingModel = "wrong:model"
  906: 	fl := newFakeLedger([]domain.UsageRecord{badCandidate})
  907: 	svc, _ := NewAutoChargeService(&fakeIdentity{token: "jwt"}, &fakeBalance{balance: ports.BillingBalance{Currency: "RUB", BalanceCents: 100}}, &fakeCharge{}, fl, testClock{time.Unix(1, 0).UTC()}, AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 1})
  908: 	_, err := svc.Run(t.Context(), AutoChargeInput{UserID: "u", BillingSubjectUserID: "billing"})
  909: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation) {
  910: 		t.Fatalf("candidate err=%v", err)
  911: 	}
  912: 
  913: 	valid := rec("store-open-bad", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  914: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{valid}, 100, time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
  915: 	if err != nil {
  916: 		t.Fatal(err)
  917: 	}
  918: 	snapshot := ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{Batch: plan.Batch, Allocations: plan.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: claimedExpected(plan)}
  919: 	snapshot.ExpectedRecords[0].BillableAt = nil
  920: 	fl = newFakeLedger(nil)
  921: 	fl.open = []ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{snapshot}
  922: 	svc, _ = NewAutoChargeService(&fakeIdentity{token: "jwt"}, &fakeBalance{balance: ports.BillingBalance{Currency: "RUB", BalanceCents: 100}}, &fakeCharge{}, fl, testClock{time.Unix(1, 0).UTC()}, AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 1})
  923: 	_, err = svc.Run(t.Context(), AutoChargeInput{UserID: "u", BillingSubjectUserID: "billing"})
  924: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation) {
  925: 		t.Fatalf("open err=%v", err)
  926: 	}
  927: }
  928: 
  929: func TestChargeBatchStateMachineIsConditionalAndIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
  930: 	r := rec("state-machine", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  931: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{r}, 100, time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
  932: 	if err != nil {
  933: 		t.Fatal(err)
  934: 	}
  935: 	fl := newFakeLedger([]domain.UsageRecord{r})
  936: 	snapshot, err := fl.PrepareChargeBatch(t.Context(), plan)
  937: 	if err != nil {
  938: 		t.Fatal(err)
  939: 	}
  940: 	chargedAt := time.Unix(2, 0).UTC()
  941: 	success := ports.UsageChargeSuccess{BatchID: snapshot.Batch.ID, ChargedAt: chargedAt, Allocations: snapshot.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: snapshot.ExpectedRecords}
  942: 	if err := fl.ApplyChargeSuccess(t.Context(), success); err != nil {
  943: 		t.Fatal(err)
  944: 	}
  945: 	record := fl.records[r.LocalRequestID]
  946: 	if err := fl.ApplyChargeSuccess(t.Context(), success); err != nil {
  947: 		t.Fatal(err)
  948: 	}
  949: 	if fl.records[r.LocalRequestID] != record {
  950: 		t.Fatal("idempotent success reapplied allocations")
  951: 	}
  952: 	if err := fl.MarkChargeBatchFailed(t.Context(), snapshot.Batch.ID, domain.BillingChargeStatusPending, "billing_unavailable", time.Unix(3, 0).UTC()); err != nil {
  953: 		t.Fatal(err)
  954: 	}
  955: 	if got := fl.batches[snapshot.Batch.ID].Batch.Status; got != domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
  956: 		t.Fatalf("succeeded overwritten as %s", got)
  957: 	}
  958: }

FILE: internal/application/billing/retry_failed_batch.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 417ab2b245ebb682d07f37d629c37ef25b761700
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package billing
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"crypto/sha256"
    6: 	"encoding/hex"
    7: 	"errors"
    8: 	"fmt"
    9: 	"time"
   10: 
   11: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
   12: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
   13: )
   14: 
   15: var (
   16: 	ErrChargeBatchNotFound  = errors.New("billing charge batch not found")
   17: 	ErrChargeBatchNotFailed = errors.New("billing charge batch is not failed")
   18: )
   19: 
   20: type FailedBatchRetryService struct {
   21: 	charge ports.BillingChargeClient
   22: 	ledger ports.AdminUsageLedger
   23: 	clock  ports.Clock
   24: }
   25: 
   26: func NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge ports.BillingChargeClient, usageLedger ports.AdminUsageLedger, clock ports.Clock) (*FailedBatchRetryService, error) {
   27: 	if charge == nil || usageLedger == nil || clock == nil {
   28: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%w: retry dependency", ErrInvalidBillingInput)
   29: 	}
   30: 	return &FailedBatchRetryService{charge: charge, ledger: usageLedger, clock: clock}, nil
   31: }
   32: 
   33: func (s *FailedBatchRetryService) RetryFailedBatch(ctx context.Context, batchID string, audit domain.AuditContext) (domain.BillingChargeBatch, error) {
   34: 	snapshot, err := s.ledger.LoadChargeBatchByID(ctx, batchID)
   35: 	if err != nil {
   36: 		if errors.Is(err, ports.ErrNotFound) {
   37: 			return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeBatchNotFound
   38: 		}
   39: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreUnavailable
   40: 	}
   41: 	if snapshot.Batch.ID != batchID {
   42: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation
   43: 	}
   44: 	if snapshot.Batch.Status != domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed {
   45: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeBatchNotFailed
   46: 	}
   47: 	if audit.Action != domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry || audit.AdminSubject != "admin_token" || audit.EntityType != "billing_charge_batch" || audit.EntityID != batchID || audit.RequestID == "" || audit.ID == "" {
   48: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation
   49: 	}
   50: 	input := AutoChargeInput{UserID: snapshot.Batch.UserID, BillingSubjectUserID: snapshot.Batch.BillingSubjectUserID, Currency: snapshot.Batch.Currency}
   51: 	if err := ValidateChargeSnapshot(input, snapshot); err != nil {
   52: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation
   53: 	}
   54: 
   55: 	now := s.clock.Now().UTC()
   56: 	if now.IsZero() || now.Location() != time.UTC {
   57: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreContractViolation
   58: 	}
   59: 
   60: 	// Persist who initiated the external financial side effect before calling
   61: 	// Billing. This audit survives invalid Billing responses and later local
   62: 	// reconciliation failures.
   63: 	attemptAudit := retryPhaseAudit(audit, "attempt", now)
   64: 	attemptAudit.BeforeState = billingRetryAuditState(snapshot.Batch)
   65: 	attemptAudit.AfterState = billingRetryAuditState(snapshot.Batch)
   66: 	if err := s.ledger.RecordChargeRetryAttemptWithAudit(ctx, snapshot, attemptAudit); err != nil {
   67: 		switch {
   68: 		case errors.Is(err, ports.ErrNotFound):
   69: 			return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeBatchNotFound
   70: 		case errors.Is(err, ports.ErrAdminStateConflict):
   71: 			return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeBatchNotFailed
   72: 		default:
   73: 			return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrBillingStoreUnavailable
   74: 		}
   75: 	}
   76: 
   77: 	chargeResult, err := s.charge.Charge(ctx, ports.BillingChargeRequest{
   78: 		RequestID:    snapshot.Batch.ID,
   79: 		UserID:       snapshot.Batch.BillingSubjectUserID,
   80: 		Model:        snapshot.Batch.BillingModel,
   81: 		InputTokens:  snapshot.Batch.InputTokens,
   82: 		OutputTokens: snapshot.Batch.OutputTokens,
   83: 		AmountCents:  snapshot.Batch.AmountCents,
   84: 		Currency:     snapshot.Batch.Currency,
   85: 	})
   86: 	if err != nil {
   87: 		failed := snapshot.Batch
   88: 		failed.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed
   89: 		failed.BillingErrorCode = "billing_unavailable"
   90: 		failed.FailedAt = &now
   91: 		failed.UpdatedAt = now
   92: 
   93: 		outcomeAudit := retryPhaseAudit(audit, "failed", now)
   94: 		outcomeAudit.BeforeState = billingRetryAuditState(snapshot.Batch)
   95: 		outcomeAudit.AfterState = billingRetryAuditState(failed)
   96: 		if markErr := s.ledger.MarkChargeRetryFailedWithAudit(ctx, snapshot.Batch.ID, domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed, "billing_unavailable", now, outcomeAudit); markErr != nil {
   97: 			return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired
   98: 		}
   99: 		return failed, ErrBillingUnavailable
  100: 	}
  101: 	if err := validateBillingChargeResult(chargeResult); err != nil {
  102: 		// The pre-call attempt audit is already durable. The externally observed
  103: 		// result is unsafe to reconcile automatically.
  104: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired
  105: 	}
  106: 
  107: 	succeeded := snapshot.Batch
  108: 	succeeded.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  109: 	succeeded.BillingResponseBalanceCents = chargeResult.BalanceCents
  110: 	succeeded.BillingErrorCode = ""
  111: 	succeeded.ChargedAt = &now
  112: 	succeeded.FailedAt = nil
  113: 	succeeded.UpdatedAt = now
  114: 
  115: 	outcomeAudit := retryPhaseAudit(audit, "succeeded", now)
  116: 	outcomeAudit.BeforeState = billingRetryAuditState(snapshot.Batch)
  117: 	outcomeAudit.AfterState = billingRetryAuditState(succeeded)
  118: 	success := ports.UsageChargeSuccess{
  119: 		BatchID:             snapshot.Batch.ID,
  120: 		BillingBalanceCents: chargeResult.BalanceCents,
  121: 		ChargedAt:           now,
  122: 		Allocations:         snapshot.Allocations,
  123: 		ExpectedRecords:     snapshot.ExpectedRecords,
  124: 	}
  125: 	if err := s.ledger.ApplyChargeRetrySuccessWithAudit(ctx, success, outcomeAudit); err != nil {
  126: 		// The attempt audit was committed before the external call, so the
  127: 		// initiator and request ID remain durable even when reconciliation fails.
  128: 		return domain.BillingChargeBatch{}, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired
  129: 	}
  130: 	return succeeded, nil
  131: }
  132: 
  133: func retryPhaseAudit(base domain.AuditContext, phase string, at time.Time) domain.AuditContext {
  134: 	sum := sha256.Sum256([]byte(base.ID + "\x00" + phase))
  135: 	base.ID = "audit_" + hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
  136: 	base.CreatedAt = at.UTC()
  137: 	return base
  138: }
  139: 
  140: func billingRetryAuditState(batch domain.BillingChargeBatch) domain.AuditState {
  141: 	return domain.AuditState{
  142: 		"id":                             batch.ID,
  143: 		"user_id":                        batch.UserID,
  144: 		"billing_subject_user_id":        batch.BillingSubjectUserID,
  145: 		"provider_type":                  batch.ProviderType,
  146: 		"client_model":                   batch.ClientModel,
  147: 		"billing_model":                  batch.BillingModel,
  148: 		"input_tokens":                   batch.InputTokens,
  149: 		"output_tokens":                  batch.OutputTokens,
  150: 		"amount_cents":                   batch.AmountCents,
  151: 		"currency":                       batch.Currency,
  152: 		"billing_status":                 batch.Status,
  153: 		"billing_response_balance_cents": batch.BillingResponseBalanceCents,
  154: 		"billing_error_code":             batch.BillingErrorCode,
  155: 		"created_at":                     batch.CreatedAt,
  156: 		"charged_at":                     batch.ChargedAt,
  157: 		"failed_at":                      batch.FailedAt,
  158: 		"updated_at":                     batch.UpdatedAt,
  159: 	}
  160: }

FILE: internal/application/billing/retry_failed_batch_test.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: e3b5726e2afcbae17ab66464034e6b9cb131d970
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package billing
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"errors"
    6: 	"reflect"
    7: 	"strings"
    8: 	"sync"
    9: 	"testing"
   10: 	"time"
   11: 
   12: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
   13: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
   14: )
   15: 
   16: type retryClock struct{ value time.Time }
   17: 
   18: func (c retryClock) Now() time.Time { return c.value }
   19: 
   20: type retryCharge struct {
   21: 	mu       sync.Mutex
   22: 	requests []ports.BillingChargeRequest
   23: 	result   ports.BillingChargeResult
   24: 	err      error
   25: }
   26: 
   27: func (f *retryCharge) Charge(_ context.Context, request ports.BillingChargeRequest) (ports.BillingChargeResult, error) {
   28: 	f.mu.Lock()
   29: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   30: 	f.requests = append(f.requests, request)
   31: 	return f.result, f.err
   32: }
   33: 
   34: type retryLedger struct {
   35: 	ports.AdminUsageLedger
   36: 	mu        sync.Mutex
   37: 	snapshot  ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot
   38: 	loadErr   error
   39: 	success   *ports.UsageChargeSuccess
   40: 	audit     domain.AuditContext
   41: 	markCalls int
   42: }
   43: 
   44: func (f *retryLedger) LoadChargeBatchByID(_ context.Context, id string) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {
   45: 	f.mu.Lock()
   46: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   47: 	if f.loadErr != nil {
   48: 		return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{}, f.loadErr
   49: 	}
   50: 	if f.snapshot.Batch.ID != id {
   51: 		return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{}, ports.ErrNotFound
   52: 	}
   53: 	return f.snapshot, nil
   54: }
   55: func (f *retryLedger) RecordChargeRetryAttemptWithAudit(_ context.Context, snapshot ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   56: 	f.mu.Lock()
   57: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   58: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(snapshot, f.snapshot) {
   59: 		return errors.New("retry snapshot changed")
   60: 	}
   61: 	f.audit = audit
   62: 	return nil
   63: }
   64: func (f *retryLedger) ApplyChargeRetrySuccessWithAudit(_ context.Context, success ports.UsageChargeSuccess, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   65: 	f.mu.Lock()
   66: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   67: 	copied := success
   68: 	f.success = &copied
   69: 	f.audit = audit
   70: 	return nil
   71: }
   72: func (f *retryLedger) MarkChargeRetryFailedWithAudit(_ context.Context, _ string, _ domain.BillingChargeStatus, _ string, _ time.Time, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   73: 	f.mu.Lock()
   74: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   75: 	f.markCalls++
   76: 	f.audit = audit
   77: 	return nil
   78: }
   79: 
   80: func failedSnapshot(t *testing.T) ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot {
   81: 	t.Helper()
   82: 	created := time.Unix(10, 0).UTC()
   83: 	billableAt := created
   84: 	record := domain.UsageRecord{LocalRequestID: "llmreq_retry", UserID: "usr_1", ProviderType: domain.ProviderOpenAI, ClientModel: "gpt-4.1-mini", BillingModel: "openai:gpt-4.1-mini", Currency: "RUB", Usage: domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 100, OutputTokens: 50}, UsageCompleteness: "detailed", Status: domain.UsageStatusBillable, ClientAmountCents: 100, RemainingAmountCents: 100, CreatedAt: created, UpdatedAt: created, BillableAt: &billableAt}
   85: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing_usr_1", []domain.UsageRecord{record}, 100, created)
   86: 	if err != nil {
   87: 		t.Fatal(err)
   88: 	}
   89: 	for i := range plan.ExpectedRecords {
   90: 		plan.ExpectedRecords[i].BillingChargeRequestID = plan.Batch.ID
   91: 	}
   92: 	failedAt := created.Add(time.Second)
   93: 	plan.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed
   94: 	plan.Batch.BillingErrorCode = "billing_unavailable"
   95: 	plan.Batch.FailedAt = &failedAt
   96: 	plan.Batch.UpdatedAt = failedAt
   97: 	return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{Batch: plan.Batch, Allocations: plan.Allocations, ExpectedRecords: plan.ExpectedRecords}
   98: }
   99: 
  100: func TestRetryFailedBatchReusesExactPersistedCommandAndAllocations(t *testing.T) {
  101: 	snapshot := failedSnapshot(t)
  102: 	balance := int64(900)
  103: 	charge := &retryCharge{result: ports.BillingChargeResult{BalanceCents: &balance}}
  104: 	ledgerStore := &retryLedger{snapshot: snapshot}
  105: 	service, err := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledgerStore, retryClock{value: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  106: 	if err != nil {
  107: 		t.Fatal(err)
  108: 	}
  109: 	audit := domain.AuditContext{ID: "audit_1", AdminSubject: "admin_token", Action: domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry, EntityType: "billing_charge_batch", EntityID: snapshot.Batch.ID, RequestID: "admreq_1", CreatedAt: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()}
  110: 	batch, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(nil, snapshot.Batch.ID, audit)
  111: 	if err != nil {
  112: 		t.Fatal(err)
  113: 	}
  114: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  115: 	requests := append([]ports.BillingChargeRequest(nil), charge.requests...)
  116: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  117: 	if len(requests) != 1 {
  118: 		t.Fatalf("requests=%d", len(requests))
  119: 	}
  120: 	expectedRequest := ports.BillingChargeRequest{RequestID: snapshot.Batch.ID, UserID: snapshot.Batch.BillingSubjectUserID, Model: snapshot.Batch.BillingModel, InputTokens: snapshot.Batch.InputTokens, OutputTokens: snapshot.Batch.OutputTokens, AmountCents: snapshot.Batch.AmountCents, Currency: snapshot.Batch.Currency}
  121: 	if requests[0] != expectedRequest {
  122: 		t.Fatalf("request=%+v expected=%+v", requests[0], expectedRequest)
  123: 	}
  124: 	ledgerStore.mu.Lock()
  125: 	success, gotAudit := ledgerStore.success, ledgerStore.audit
  126: 	ledgerStore.mu.Unlock()
  127: 	if success == nil || success.BatchID != snapshot.Batch.ID || !reflect.DeepEqual(success.Allocations, snapshot.Allocations) || !reflect.DeepEqual(success.ExpectedRecords, snapshot.ExpectedRecords) {
  128: 		t.Fatalf("success=%+v", success)
  129: 	}
  130: 	if gotAudit.Action != domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry || gotAudit.AfterState["billing_status"] != domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
  131: 		t.Fatalf("audit=%+v", gotAudit)
  132: 	}
  133: 	if batch.Status != domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded || batch.ID != snapshot.Batch.ID {
  134: 		t.Fatalf("batch=%+v", batch)
  135: 	}
  136: }
  137: 
  138: func TestRetrySucceededBatchIsRejectedWithoutBillingCall(t *testing.T) {
  139: 	snapshot := failedSnapshot(t)
  140: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  141: 	snapshot.Batch.BillingErrorCode = ""
  142: 	snapshot.Batch.FailedAt = nil
  143: 	chargedAt := time.Unix(30, 0).UTC()
  144: 	snapshot.Batch.ChargedAt = &chargedAt
  145: 	snapshot.Batch.UpdatedAt = chargedAt
  146: 	charge := &retryCharge{}
  147: 	service, err := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, &retryLedger{snapshot: snapshot}, retryClock{value: chargedAt})
  148: 	if err != nil {
  149: 		t.Fatal(err)
  150: 	}
  151: 	_, err = service.RetryFailedBatch(nil, snapshot.Batch.ID, domain.AuditContext{})
  152: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeBatchNotFailed) {
  153: 		t.Fatalf("err=%v", err)
  154: 	}
  155: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  156: 	calls := len(charge.requests)
  157: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  158: 	if calls != 0 {
  159: 		t.Fatalf("charge calls=%d", calls)
  160: 	}
  161: }

   72: func (f *stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake) LoadChargeBatchByID(context.Context, string) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {
   73: 	f.mu.Lock()
   74: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   75: 	return f.snapshot, f.loadErr
   76: }
   77: func (f *stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake) RecordChargeRetryAttemptWithAudit(_ context.Context, snapshot ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   78: 	f.mu.Lock()
   79: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   80: 	f.attemptCalls++
   81: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(snapshot, f.snapshot) {
   82: 		return errors.New("snapshot changed")
   83: 	}
   84: 	f.attemptAudit = audit
   85: 	return f.attemptErr
   86: }
   87: func (f *stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake) ApplyChargeRetrySuccessWithAudit(_ context.Context, success ports.UsageChargeSuccess, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   88: 	f.mu.Lock()
   89: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   90: 	f.applyCalls++
   91: 	f.success = success
   92: 	f.applyAudit = audit
   93: 	return f.applyErr
   94: }
   95: func (f *stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake) MarkChargeRetryFailedWithAudit(context.Context, string, domain.BillingChargeStatus, string, time.Time, domain.AuditContext) error {
   96: 	f.mu.Lock()
   97: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   98: 	f.markCalls++
   99: 	return f.markErr
  100: }
  101: 
  102: type stage10MajorRetryChargeFake struct {
  103: 	mu      sync.Mutex
  104: 	ledger  *stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake
  105: 	result  ports.BillingChargeResult
  106: 	err     error
  107: 	request ports.BillingChargeRequest
  108: 	calls   int
  109: }
  110: 
  111: func (f *stage10MajorRetryChargeFake) Charge(_ context.Context, request ports.BillingChargeRequest) (ports.BillingChargeResult, error) {
  112: 	f.mu.Lock()
  113: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
  114: 	if !f.ledger.attempted() {
  115: 		return ports.BillingChargeResult{}, errors.New("charge called before durable audit")
  116: 	}
  117: 	f.calls++
  118: 	f.request = request
  119: 	return f.result, f.err
  120: }
  121: 
  122: type stage10MajorRetryClock struct {
  123: 	mu  sync.Mutex
  124: 	now time.Time
  125: }
  126: 
  127: func (f *stage10MajorRetryClock) Now() time.Time {
  128: 	f.mu.Lock()
  129: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
  130: 	return f.now
  131: }

  133: func stage10MajorFailedRetrySnapshot(t *testing.T) ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot {
  134: 	t.Helper()
  135: 	at := time.Unix(10, 0).UTC()
  136: 	billableAt := at
  137: 	record := domain.UsageRecord{
  138: 		LocalRequestID:       "llmreq_retry",
  139: 		UserID:               "usr_1",
  140: 		ProviderType:         domain.ProviderOpenAI,
  141: 		ClientModel:          "model-a",
  142: 		BillingModel:         "openai:model-a",
  143: 		Usage:                domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 100, OutputTokens: 50},
  144: 		UsageCompleteness:    "detailed",
  145: 		ClientAmountCents:    10,
  146: 		RemainingAmountCents: 10,
  147: 		Currency:             "RUB",
  148: 		Status:               domain.UsageStatusBillable,
  149: 		CreatedAt:            at,
  150: 		BillableAt:           &billableAt,
  151: 		UpdatedAt:            at,
  152: 	}
  153: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing_usr_1", []domain.UsageRecord{record}, 10, at)
  154: 	if err != nil {
  155: 		t.Fatal(err)
  156: 	}
  157: 	expected := append([]domain.UsageRecord(nil), plan.ExpectedRecords...)
  158: 	for i := range expected {
  159: 		expected[i].BillingChargeRequestID = plan.Batch.ID
  160: 	}
  161: 	failedAt := at.Add(time.Second)
  162: 	batch := plan.Batch
  163: 	batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed
  164: 	batch.BillingErrorCode = "billing_unavailable"
  165: 	batch.FailedAt = &failedAt
  166: 	batch.UpdatedAt = failedAt
  167: 	return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{
  168: 		Batch:           batch,
  169: 		Allocations:     append([]domain.BillingChargeAllocation(nil), plan.Allocations...),
  170: 		ExpectedRecords: expected,
  171: 	}
  172: }
  173: 
  174: func stage10MajorRetryAudit(batchID string) domain.AuditContext {
  175: 	return domain.AuditContext{
  176: 		ID:           "audit_base",
  177: 		AdminSubject: "admin_token",
  178: 		Action:       domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry,
  179: 		EntityType:   "billing_charge_batch",
  180: 		EntityID:     batchID,
  181: 		RequestID:    "admreq_retry",
  182: 	}
  183: }
  184: 
  185: func TestStage10MajorRetryFailedBatchAuditsBeforeChargeAndReusesPersistedCommand(t *testing.T) {
  186: 	snapshot := stage10MajorFailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  187: 	ledger := &stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  188: 	charge := &stage10MajorRetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger}
  189: 	service, err := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10MajorRetryClock{now: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  190: 	if err != nil {
  191: 		t.Fatal(err)
  192: 	}
  193: 	result, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10MajorRetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  194: 	if err != nil {
  195: 		t.Fatal(err)
  196: 	}
  197: 	if result.Status != domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
  198: 		t.Fatalf("result = %+v", result)
  199: 	}
  200: 
  201: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  202: 	request := charge.request
  203: 	calls := charge.calls
  204: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  205: 	if calls != 1 {
  206: 		t.Fatalf("charge calls = %d", calls)
  207: 	}
  208: 	wantRequest := ports.BillingChargeRequest{
  209: 		RequestID:    snapshot.Batch.ID,
  210: 		UserID:       snapshot.Batch.BillingSubjectUserID,
  211: 		Model:        snapshot.Batch.BillingModel,
  212: 		InputTokens:  snapshot.Batch.InputTokens,
  213: 		OutputTokens: snapshot.Batch.OutputTokens,
  214: 		AmountCents:  snapshot.Batch.AmountCents,
  215: 		Currency:     snapshot.Batch.Currency,
  216: 	}
  217: 	if request != wantRequest {
  218: 		t.Fatalf("request = %+v, want %+v", request, wantRequest)
  219: 	}
  220: 
  221: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  222: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()
  223: 	if ledger.attemptCalls != 1 || ledger.applyCalls != 1 {
  224: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/apply calls = %d/%d", ledger.attemptCalls, ledger.applyCalls)
  225: 	}
  226: 	if ledger.attemptAudit.Action != domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry || ledger.attemptAudit.RequestID != "admreq_retry" {
  227: 		t.Fatalf("attempt audit = %+v", ledger.attemptAudit)
  228: 	}
  229: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(ledger.success.Allocations, snapshot.Allocations) || !reflect.DeepEqual(ledger.success.ExpectedRecords, snapshot.ExpectedRecords) {
  230: 		t.Fatal("persisted allocations or expectations were rebuilt")
  231: 	}
  232: }
  233: 
  234: func TestStage10MajorRetryAttemptAuditSurvivesInvalidBillingResult(t *testing.T) {
  235: 	snapshot := stage10MajorFailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  236: 	negative := int64(-1)
  237: 	ledger := &stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  238: 	charge := &stage10MajorRetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger, result: ports.BillingChargeResult{BalanceCents: &negative}}
  239: 	service, _ := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10MajorRetryClock{now: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  240: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10MajorRetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  241: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired) {
  242: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  243: 	}
  244: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  245: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()
  246: 	if ledger.attemptCalls != 1 || ledger.applyCalls != 0 {
  247: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/apply calls = %d/%d", ledger.attemptCalls, ledger.applyCalls)
  248: 	}
  249: }
  250: 
  251: func TestStage10MajorRetryAttemptAuditSurvivesReconciliationFailure(t *testing.T) {
  252: 	snapshot := stage10MajorFailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  253: 	ledger := &stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot, applyErr: errors.New("reconciliation conflict")}
  254: 	charge := &stage10MajorRetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger}
  255: 	service, _ := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10MajorRetryClock{now: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  256: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10MajorRetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  257: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired) {
  258: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  259: 	}
  260: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  261: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()
  262: 	if ledger.attemptCalls != 1 || ledger.applyCalls != 1 {
  263: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/apply calls = %d/%d", ledger.attemptCalls, ledger.applyCalls)
  264: 	}
  265: }
  266: 
  267: func TestStage10MajorRetrySucceededBatchIsRejectedBeforeAuditOrCharge(t *testing.T) {
  268: 	snapshot := stage10MajorFailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  269: 	chargedAt := time.Unix(30, 0).UTC()
  270: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  271: 	snapshot.Batch.ChargedAt = &chargedAt
  272: 	snapshot.Batch.FailedAt = nil
  273: 	snapshot.Batch.BillingErrorCode = ""
  274: 	snapshot.Batch.UpdatedAt = chargedAt
  275: 	ledger := &stage10MajorRetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  276: 	charge := &stage10MajorRetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger}
  277: 	service, _ := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10MajorRetryClock{now: time.Unix(40, 0).UTC()})
  278: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10MajorRetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  279: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeBatchNotFailed) {
  280: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  281: 	}
  282: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  283: 	attemptCalls := ledger.attemptCalls
  284: 	ledger.mu.Unlock()
  285: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  286: 	chargeCalls := charge.calls
  287: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  288: 	if attemptCalls != 0 || chargeCalls != 0 {
  289: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/charge calls = %d/%d", attemptCalls, chargeCalls)
  290: 	}
  291: }

   68: func stage10V5PendingSnapshot(t *testing.T, model string, createdAt time.Time, amount int64) ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot {
   69: 	t.Helper()
   70: 	billableAt := createdAt
   71: 	record := domain.UsageRecord{
   72: 		LocalRequestID:       "llmreq_" + model,
   73: 		UserID:               "usr_1",
   74: 		APIKeyID:             "ak_1",
   75: 		APIFamily:            domain.APIFamilyOpenAICompatible,
   76: 		EndpointKind:         domain.EndpointChat,
   77: 		ClientModel:          model,
   78: 		BillingModel:         "openai:" + model,
   79: 		SelectedRouteID:      "route_" + model,
   80: 		SelectedResellerID:   "reseller_1",
   81: 		ProviderType:         domain.ProviderOpenAI,
   82: 		ProviderModel:        model,
   83: 		Usage:                domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 100, OutputTokens: 50},
   84: 		UsageCompleteness:    "detailed",
   85: 		ClientAmountCents:    amount,
   86: 		RemainingAmountCents: amount,
   87: 		Currency:             "RUB",
   88: 		Status:               domain.UsageStatusBillable,
   89: 		CreatedAt:            createdAt,
   90: 		BillableAt:           &billableAt,
   91: 		UpdatedAt:            createdAt,
   92: 	}
   93: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing_usr_1", []domain.UsageRecord{record}, amount, createdAt)
   94: 	if err != nil {
   95: 		t.Fatal(err)
   96: 	}
   97: 	for i := range plan.ExpectedRecords {
   98: 		plan.ExpectedRecords[i].BillingChargeRequestID = plan.Batch.ID
   99: 	}
  100: 	return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{
  101: 		Batch:           plan.Batch,
  102: 		Allocations:     append([]domain.BillingChargeAllocation(nil), plan.Allocations...),
  103: 		ExpectedRecords: append([]domain.UsageRecord(nil), plan.ExpectedRecords...),
  104: 	}
  105: }
  106: 
  107: func TestStage10V5OpenBatchesAreProcessedByCreatedAtThenBatchID(t *testing.T) {
  108: 	firstTime := time.Unix(10, 0).UTC()
  109: 	secondTime := time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()
  110: 	snapshots := []ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{
  111: 		stage10V5PendingSnapshot(t, "model-c", secondTime, 30),
  112: 		stage10V5PendingSnapshot(t, "model-b", firstTime, 20),
  113: 		stage10V5PendingSnapshot(t, "model-a", firstTime, 10),
  114: 	}
  115: 	expected := append([]ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot(nil), snapshots...)
  116: 	sort.Slice(expected, func(i, j int) bool {
  117: 		if !expected[i].Batch.CreatedAt.Equal(expected[j].Batch.CreatedAt) {
  118: 			return expected[i].Batch.CreatedAt.Before(expected[j].Batch.CreatedAt)
  119: 		}
  120: 		return expected[i].Batch.ID < expected[j].Batch.ID
  121: 	})
  122: 
  123: 	ledger := &stage10V5OpenBatchLedger{open: snapshots}
  124: 	charge := &stage10V5OpenBatchCharge{}
  125: 	service, err := NewAutoChargeService(
  126: 		stage10V5UnusedIdentity{},
  127: 		stage10V5UnusedBalance{},
  128: 		charge,
  129: 		ledger,
  130: 		stage10V5AutoChargeClock{now: time.Unix(30, 0).UTC()},
  131: 		AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 1},
  132: 	)
  133: 	if err != nil {
  134: 		t.Fatal(err)
  135: 	}
  136: 	result, err := service.Run(context.Background(), AutoChargeInput{
  137: 		UserID:               "usr_1",
  138: 		BillingSubjectUserID: "billing_usr_1",
  139: 		Currency:             "RUB",
  140: 	})
  141: 	if err != nil {
  142: 		t.Fatal(err)
  143: 	}
  144: 
  145: 	wantIDs := make([]string, len(expected))
  146: 	for i := range expected {
  147: 		wantIDs[i] = expected[i].Batch.ID
  148: 	}
  149: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  150: 	gotRequests := append([]ports.BillingChargeRequest(nil), charge.requests...)
  151: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  152: 	gotIDs := make([]string, len(gotRequests))
  153: 	for i := range gotRequests {
  154: 		gotIDs[i] = gotRequests[i].RequestID
  155: 	}
  156: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(gotIDs, wantIDs) {
  157: 		t.Fatalf("charge order = %v, want %v", gotIDs, wantIDs)
  158: 	}
  159: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(result.ProcessedBatchIDs, wantIDs) {
  160: 		t.Fatalf("result order = %v, want %v", result.ProcessedBatchIDs, wantIDs)
  161: 	}
  162: }

   66: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) ResolvePricingFailedWithAudit(context.Context, domain.UsageRecord, domain.UsageRecord, domain.AuditContext) (ports.UsageTransitionResult, error) {
   67: 	return ports.UsageTransitionResult{}, nil
   68: }
   69: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) ListBillingChargeBatches(context.Context, ports.BillingChargeBatchListFilter) (ports.Page[domain.BillingChargeBatch], error) {
   70: 	return ports.Page[domain.BillingChargeBatch]{}, nil
   71: }
   72: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) LoadChargeBatchByID(context.Context, string) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {
   73: 	f.mu.Lock()
   74: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   75: 	return f.snapshot, f.loadErr
   76: }
   77: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) RecordChargeRetryAttemptWithAudit(_ context.Context, snapshot ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   78: 	f.mu.Lock()
   79: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   80: 	f.attemptCalls++
   81: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(snapshot, f.snapshot) {
   82: 		return errors.New("snapshot changed")
   83: 	}
   84: 	f.attemptAudit = audit
   85: 	return f.attemptErr
   86: }
   87: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) ApplyChargeRetrySuccessWithAudit(_ context.Context, success ports.UsageChargeSuccess, audit domain.AuditContext) error {
   88: 	f.mu.Lock()
   89: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   90: 	f.applyCalls++
   91: 	f.success = success
   92: 	f.applyAudit = audit
   93: 	return f.applyErr
   94: }
   95: func (f *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake) MarkChargeRetryFailedWithAudit(context.Context, string, domain.BillingChargeStatus, string, time.Time, domain.AuditContext) error {
   96: 	f.mu.Lock()
   97: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   98: 	f.markCalls++
   99: 	return f.markErr
  100: }
  101: 
  102: type stage10V5RetryChargeFake struct {
  103: 	mu      sync.Mutex
  104: 	ledger  *stage10V5RetryLedgerFake
  105: 	result  ports.BillingChargeResult
  106: 	err     error
  107: 	request ports.BillingChargeRequest
  108: 	calls   int
  109: }
  110: 
  111: func (f *stage10V5RetryChargeFake) Charge(_ context.Context, request ports.BillingChargeRequest) (ports.BillingChargeResult, error) {
  112: 	f.mu.Lock()
  113: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
  114: 	if !f.ledger.attempted() {
  115: 		return ports.BillingChargeResult{}, errors.New("charge called before durable audit")
  116: 	}
  117: 	f.calls++
  118: 	f.request = request
  119: 	return f.result, f.err
  120: }
  121: 
  122: type stage10V5RetryClock struct {
  123: 	mu  sync.Mutex
  124: 	now time.Time
  125: }
  126: 
  127: func (f *stage10V5RetryClock) Now() time.Time {
  128: 	f.mu.Lock()
  129: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
  130: 	return f.now
  131: }
  132: 
  133: func stage10V5FailedRetrySnapshot(t *testing.T) ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot {
  134: 	t.Helper()
  135: 	at := time.Unix(10, 0).UTC()
  136: 	billableAt := at
  137: 	record := domain.UsageRecord{
  138: 		LocalRequestID:       "llmreq_retry",
  139: 		UserID:               "usr_1",
  140: 		ProviderType:         domain.ProviderOpenAI,
  141: 		ClientModel:          "model-a",
  142: 		BillingModel:         "openai:model-a",
  143: 		Usage:                domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 100, OutputTokens: 50},
  144: 		UsageCompleteness:    "detailed",
  145: 		ClientAmountCents:    10,
  146: 		RemainingAmountCents: 10,
  147: 		Currency:             "RUB",
  148: 		Status:               domain.UsageStatusBillable,
  149: 		CreatedAt:            at,
  150: 		BillableAt:           &billableAt,
  151: 		UpdatedAt:            at,
  152: 	}
  153: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing_usr_1", []domain.UsageRecord{record}, 10, at)
  154: 	if err != nil {
  155: 		t.Fatal(err)
  156: 	}
  157: 	expected := append([]domain.UsageRecord(nil), plan.ExpectedRecords...)
  158: 	for i := range expected {
  159: 		expected[i].BillingChargeRequestID = plan.Batch.ID
  160: 	}
  161: 	failedAt := at.Add(time.Second)
  162: 	batch := plan.Batch
  163: 	batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed
  164: 	batch.BillingErrorCode = "billing_unavailable"
  165: 	batch.FailedAt = &failedAt
  166: 	batch.UpdatedAt = failedAt
  167: 	return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{
  168: 		Batch:           batch,
  169: 		Allocations:     append([]domain.BillingChargeAllocation(nil), plan.Allocations...),
  170: 		ExpectedRecords: expected,
  171: 	}
  172: }
  173: 
  174: func stage10V5RetryAudit(batchID string) domain.AuditContext {
  175: 	return domain.AuditContext{
  176: 		ID:           "audit_base",
  177: 		AdminSubject: "admin_token",
  178: 		Action:       domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry,
  179: 		EntityType:   "billing_charge_batch",
  180: 		EntityID:     batchID,
  181: 		RequestID:    "admreq_retry",
  182: 	}
  183: }
  184: 
  185: func TestStage10V5RetryFailedBatchAuditsBeforeChargeAndReusesPersistedCommand(t *testing.T) {
  186: 	snapshot := stage10V5FailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  187: 	ledger := &stage10V5RetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  188: 	charge := &stage10V5RetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger}
  189: 	service, err := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10V5RetryClock{now: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  190: 	if err != nil {
  191: 		t.Fatal(err)
  192: 	}
  193: 	result, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10V5RetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  194: 	if err != nil {
  195: 		t.Fatal(err)
  196: 	}
  197: 	if result.Status != domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
  198: 		t.Fatalf("result = %+v", result)
  199: 	}
  200: 
  201: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  202: 	request := charge.request
  203: 	calls := charge.calls
  204: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  205: 	if calls != 1 {
  206: 		t.Fatalf("charge calls = %d", calls)
  207: 	}
  208: 	wantRequest := ports.BillingChargeRequest{
  209: 		RequestID:    snapshot.Batch.ID,
  210: 		UserID:       snapshot.Batch.BillingSubjectUserID,
  211: 		Model:        snapshot.Batch.BillingModel,
  212: 		InputTokens:  snapshot.Batch.InputTokens,
  213: 		OutputTokens: snapshot.Batch.OutputTokens,
  214: 		AmountCents:  snapshot.Batch.AmountCents,
  215: 		Currency:     snapshot.Batch.Currency,
  216: 	}
  217: 	if request != wantRequest {
  218: 		t.Fatalf("request = %+v, want %+v", request, wantRequest)
  219: 	}
  220: 
  221: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  222: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()
  223: 	if ledger.attemptCalls != 1 || ledger.applyCalls != 1 {
  224: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/apply calls = %d/%d", ledger.attemptCalls, ledger.applyCalls)
  225: 	}
  226: 	if ledger.attemptAudit.Action != domain.AuditActionBillingChargeRetry || ledger.attemptAudit.RequestID != "admreq_retry" {
  227: 		t.Fatalf("attempt audit = %+v", ledger.attemptAudit)
  228: 	}
  229: 	if !reflect.DeepEqual(ledger.success.Allocations, snapshot.Allocations) || !reflect.DeepEqual(ledger.success.ExpectedRecords, snapshot.ExpectedRecords) {
  230: 		t.Fatal("persisted allocations or expectations were rebuilt")
  231: 	}
  232: }
  233: 
  234: func TestStage10V5RetryAttemptAuditSurvivesInvalidBillingResult(t *testing.T) {
  235: 	snapshot := stage10V5FailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  236: 	negative := int64(-1)
  237: 	ledger := &stage10V5RetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  238: 	charge := &stage10V5RetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger, result: ports.BillingChargeResult{BalanceCents: &negative}}
  239: 	service, _ := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10V5RetryClock{now: time.Unix(20, 0).UTC()})
  240: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10V5RetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  241: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired) {
  242: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  243: 	}
  244: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  245: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()
  246: 	if ledger.attemptCalls != 1 || ledger.applyCalls != 0 {
  247: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/apply calls = %d/%d", ledger.attemptCalls, ledger.applyCalls)
  248: 	}
  249: }

  256: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10V5RetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  257: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeReconciliationRequired) {
  258: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  259: 	}
  260: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  261: 	defer ledger.mu.Unlock()

  264: 	}

  267: func TestStage10V5RetrySucceededBatchIsRejectedBeforeAuditOrCharge(t *testing.T) {
  268: 	snapshot := stage10V5FailedRetrySnapshot(t)
  269: 	chargedAt := time.Unix(30, 0).UTC()
  270: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  271: 	snapshot.Batch.ChargedAt = &chargedAt
  272: 	snapshot.Batch.FailedAt = nil
  273: 	snapshot.Batch.BillingErrorCode = ""
  274: 	snapshot.Batch.UpdatedAt = chargedAt
  275: 	ledger := &stage10V5RetryLedgerFake{snapshot: snapshot}
  276: 	charge := &stage10V5RetryChargeFake{ledger: ledger}
  277: 	service, _ := NewFailedBatchRetryService(charge, ledger, &stage10V5RetryClock{now: time.Unix(40, 0).UTC()})
  278: 	_, err := service.RetryFailedBatch(context.Background(), snapshot.Batch.ID, stage10V5RetryAudit(snapshot.Batch.ID))
  279: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeBatchNotFailed) {
  280: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v", err)
  281: 	}
  282: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  283: 	attemptCalls := ledger.attemptCalls
  284: 	ledger.mu.Unlock()
  285: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  286: 	chargeCalls := charge.calls
  287: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  288: 	if attemptCalls != 0 || chargeCalls != 0 {
  289: 		t.Fatalf("attempt/charge calls = %d/%d", attemptCalls, chargeCalls)
  290: 	}
  291: }

   94: func TestStage10V9ZeroMinimumChargeIsAcceptedAndZeroAmountIsDeferred(t *testing.T) {
   95: 	at := time.Unix(900, 0).UTC()
   96: 	ledger := &stage10V9ZeroMinimumLedger{candidates: []domain.UsageRecord{stage10V9BillableCandidate(at)}}
   97: 	charge := &stage10V9ZeroMinimumCharge{}
   98: 
   99: 	service, err := NewAutoChargeService(
  100: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumIdentity{},
  101: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumBalance{},
  102: 		charge,
  103: 		ledger,
  104: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumClock{now: at},
  105: 		AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 0},
  106: 	)
  107: 	if err != nil {
  108: 		t.Fatalf("zero minimum charge must be accepted: %v", err)
  109: 	}
  110: 
  111: 	result, err := service.Run(context.Background(), AutoChargeInput{
  112: 		UserID:               "usr_stage10_v9",
  113: 		BillingSubjectUserID: "billing_usr_stage10_v9",
  114: 		Currency:             "RUB",
  115: 	})
  116: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrChargeDeferred) {
  117: 		t.Fatalf("Run error = %v, want ErrChargeDeferred", err)
  118: 	}
  119: 	if !result.Deferred || len(result.ProcessedBatchIDs) != 0 || result.ChargedAmountCents != 0 {
  120: 		t.Fatalf("result = %+v, want deferred result without processed batches", result)
  121: 	}
  122: 
  123: 	ledger.mu.Lock()
  124: 	prepareCalls := ledger.prepareCalls
  125: 	ledger.mu.Unlock()
  126: 	charge.mu.Lock()
  127: 	chargeCalls := charge.calls
  128: 	charge.mu.Unlock()
  129: 	if prepareCalls != 0 || chargeCalls != 0 {
  130: 		t.Fatalf("prepare calls = %d, charge calls = %d; want both zero", prepareCalls, chargeCalls)
  131: 	}
  132: 
  133: 	_, err = NewAutoChargeService(
  134: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumIdentity{},
  135: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumBalance{},
  136: 		charge,
  137: 		ledger,
  138: 		stage10V9ZeroMinimumClock{now: at},
  139: 		AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: -1},
  140: 	)
  141: 	if !errors.Is(err, ErrInvalidBillingInput) {
  142: 		t.Fatalf("negative minimum charge error = %v, want ErrInvalidBillingInput", err)
  143: 	}
  144: }

   13: type stage11ASequenceBalanceClient struct {
   14: 	responses []ports.BillingBalance
   15: 	err       error
   16: 	calls     int
   17: }
   18: 
   19: func (c *stage11ASequenceBalanceClient) GetBalance(
   20: 	_ context.Context,
   21: 	_ string,
   22: ) (ports.BillingBalance, error) {
   23: 	c.calls++
   24: 	if c.err != nil {
   25: 		return ports.BillingBalance{}, c.err
   26: 	}
   27: 	if len(c.responses) == 0 {
   28: 		return ports.BillingBalance{}, errors.New("unexpected balance call")
   29: 	}
   30: 	response := c.responses[0]
   31: 	c.responses = c.responses[1:]
   32: 	return response, nil
   33: }

   45: 		t.Context(),

   47: 		100,

   52: 			},

  205: ) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {

  219: ) error {

  221: 	return nil
  222: }

  225: 	_ context.Context,
  226: 	_ ports.UsageChargeSuccess,
  227: ) error {
  228: 	l.applyCalls++
  229: 	return nil
  230: }

  232: func TestRunDoesNotRefreshBalanceAfterFinalSucceededReplayGroup(t *testing.T) {
  233: 	now := time.Unix(1_700, 0).UTC()
  234: 	record := rec("final-succeeded-replay", domain.ProviderOpenAI, "m", 100, 0, 100, 1)
  235: 	plan, err := BuildChargePlan("billing", []domain.UsageRecord{record}, 100, now)
  236: 	if err != nil {
  237: 		t.Fatal(err)
  238: 	}
  239: 
  240: 	snapshot := ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{
  241: 		Batch:           plan.Batch,
  242: 		Allocations:     append([]domain.BillingChargeAllocation(nil), plan.Allocations...),
  243: 		ExpectedRecords: claimedExpected(plan),
  244: 	}
  245: 	snapshot.Batch.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
  246: 	snapshot.Batch.ChargedAt = &now
  247: 	snapshot.Batch.UpdatedAt = now
  248: 
  249: 	ledger := &stage11AFinalReplayLedger{candidate: record, snapshot: snapshot}
  250: 	balance := &stage11ASequenceBalanceClient{
  251: 		responses: []ports.BillingBalance{{Currency: currencyRUB, BalanceCents: 100}},
  252: 	}
  253: 	charge := &fakeCharge{}
  254: 	service, err := NewAutoChargeService(
  255: 		&fakeIdentity{token: "billing-jwt"},
  256: 		balance,
  257: 		charge,
  258: 		ledger,
  259: 		testClock{t: now},
  260: 		AutoChargeConfig{ThresholdCents: 1, MinimumChargeCents: 1},
  261: 	)
  262: 	if err != nil {
  263: 		t.Fatal(err)
  264: 	}
  265: 
  266: 	result, err := service.Run(t.Context(), AutoChargeInput{
  267: 		UserID:               "u",
  268: 		BillingSubjectUserID: "billing",
  269: 		Currency:             currencyRUB,
  270: 	})
  271: 	if err != nil {
  272: 		t.Fatalf("Run returned error after final succeeded replay: %v", err)
  273: 	}
  274: 	if balance.calls != 1 {
  275: 		t.Fatalf("balance calls = %d, want initial call only", balance.calls)
  276: 	}
  277: 	if len(charge.requests) != 0 {
  278: 		t.Fatalf("billing charge called for succeeded replay: %+v", charge.requests)
  279: 	}
  280: 	if ledger.prepareCalls != 1 || ledger.markFailedCalls != 0 || ledger.applyCalls != 0 {
  281: 		t.Fatalf(
  282: 			"ledger calls prepare=%d mark_failed=%d apply=%d",
  283: 			ledger.prepareCalls,
  284: 			ledger.markFailedCalls,
  285: 			ledger.applyCalls,
  286: 		)
  287: 	}
  288: 	if len(result.ProcessedBatchIDs) != 1 || result.ProcessedBatchIDs[0] != snapshot.Batch.ID {
  289: 		t.Fatalf("processed batches = %+v", result.ProcessedBatchIDs)
  290: 	}
  291: }

FILE: internal/application/forwarding/failure.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 289a17f8d257f5dcee6566879c1fcb429540d606
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package forwarding
    2: 
    3: import "fmt"
    4: 
    5: type FailureKind string
    6: 
    7: const (
    8: 	FailureKindRequestError                FailureKind = "request_error"
    9: 	FailureKindAuthError                   FailureKind = "auth_error"
   10: 	FailureKindRateLimited                 FailureKind = "rate_limited"
   11: 	FailureKindQuotaExceeded               FailureKind = "quota_exceeded"
   12: 	FailureKindInsufficientResellerBalance FailureKind = "insufficient_reseller_balance"
   13: 	FailureKindServerError                 FailureKind = "server_error"
   14: 	FailureKindUnavailable                 FailureKind = "unavailable"
   15: 	FailureKindUnexpectedResponse          FailureKind = "unexpected_response"
   16: 	FailureKindResponseTooLarge            FailureKind = "response_too_large"
   17: 	FailureKindInvalidAdapterInput         FailureKind = "invalid_adapter_input"
   18: )
   19: 
   20: type AttemptState string
   21: 
   22: const (
   23: 	AttemptStateNotSent          AttemptState = "not_sent"
   24: 	AttemptStateSentNoResponse   AttemptState = "sent_no_response"
   25: 	AttemptStateResponseReceived AttemptState = "response_received"
   26: )
   27: 
   28: type Classification struct {
   29: 	Kind FailureKind
   30: 
   31: 	RouteRetryCandidate bool
   32: }
   33: 
   34: type Failure struct {
   35: 	Kind FailureKind
   36: 
   37: 	StatusCode   int
   38: 	AttemptState AttemptState
   39: 
   40: 	RouteRetryCandidate bool
   41: 
   42: 	cause error
   43: }
   44: 
   45: func NewFailure(kind FailureKind, statusCode int, attemptState AttemptState, routeRetryCandidate bool, cause error) *Failure {
   46: 	return &Failure{
   47: 		Kind:                kind,
   48: 		StatusCode:          statusCode,
   49: 		AttemptState:        attemptState,
   50: 		RouteRetryCandidate: routeRetryCandidate,
   51: 		cause:               cause,
   52: 	}
   53: }
   54: 
   55: func (f *Failure) Error() string {
   56: 	if f == nil {
   57: 		return "forwarding failure"
   58: 	}
   59: 	if f.StatusCode > 0 {
   60: 		return fmt.Sprintf("forwarding failure: kind=%s status=%d attempt_state=%s route_retry_candidate=%t", f.Kind, f.StatusCode, f.AttemptState, f.RouteRetryCandidate)
   61: 	}
   62: 	return fmt.Sprintf("forwarding failure: kind=%s attempt_state=%s route_retry_candidate=%t", f.Kind, f.AttemptState, f.RouteRetryCandidate)
   63: }
   64: 
   65: func (f *Failure) FailureKindValue() string {
   66: 	if f == nil {
   67: 		return ""
   68: 	}
   69: 	return string(f.Kind)
   70: }
   71: 
   72: func (f *Failure) FailureStatusCode() int {
   73: 	if f == nil {
   74: 		return 0
   75: 	}
   76: 	return f.StatusCode
   77: }
   78: 
   79: func (f *Failure) FailureAttemptStateValue() string {
   80: 	if f == nil {
   81: 		return ""
   82: 	}
   83: 	return string(f.AttemptState)
   84: }
   85: 
   86: func (f *Failure) FailureRouteRetryCandidate() bool {
   87: 	return f != nil && f.RouteRetryCandidate
   88: }
   89: 
   90: func (f *Failure) Unwrap() error {
   91: 	if f == nil {
   92: 		return nil
   93: 	}
   94: 	return f.cause
   95: }

   10: 
   11: func TestFailureContract(t *testing.T) {
   12: 	cause := errors.New("internal cause")
   13: 	err := NewFailure(FailureKindServerError, 502, AttemptStateResponseReceived, true, cause)
   14: 	var failure *Failure
   15: 	if !errors.As(err, &failure) || failure.Kind != FailureKindServerError || failure.AttemptState != AttemptStateResponseReceived || !failure.RouteRetryCandidate {
   16: 		t.Fatalf("unexpected failure: %#v", failure)
   17: 	}
   18: 	if !errors.Is(err, cause) {
   19: 		t.Fatal("cause is not wrapped")
   20: 	}
   21: 	for _, secret := range []string{"route-123", "reseller-123", "APIKeyEnv", "Authorization", "request-body", "response-body", "sk_secret"} {
   22: 		if strings.Contains(fmt.Sprint(err), secret) {
   23: 			t.Fatalf("Error() leaked %q: %q", secret, err.Error())
   24: 		}
   25: 	}
   26: }
   27: 
   28: func TestStableValues(t *testing.T) {
   29: 	kinds := map[FailureKind]string{
   30: 		FailureKindRequestError:                "request_error",
   31: 		FailureKindAuthError:                   "auth_error",
   32: 		FailureKindRateLimited:                 "rate_limited",
   33: 		FailureKindQuotaExceeded:               "quota_exceeded",
   34: 		FailureKindInsufficientResellerBalance: "insufficient_reseller_balance",
   35: 		FailureKindServerError:                 "server_error",
   36: 		FailureKindUnavailable:                 "unavailable",
   37: 		FailureKindUnexpectedResponse:          "unexpected_response",
   38: 		FailureKindResponseTooLarge:            "response_too_large",
   39: 		FailureKindInvalidAdapterInput:         "invalid_adapter_input",
   40: 	}
   41: 	for got, want := range kinds {
   42: 		if string(got) != want {
   43: 			t.Fatalf("kind = %q want %q", got, want)
   44: 		}
   45: 	}
   46: 	states := map[AttemptState]string{
   47: 		AttemptStateNotSent:          "not_sent",
   48: 		AttemptStateSentNoResponse:   "sent_no_response",
   49: 		AttemptStateResponseReceived: "response_received",
   50: 	}
   51: 	for got, want := range states {
   52: 		if string(got) != want {
   53: 			t.Fatalf("state = %q want %q", got, want)
   54: 		}
   55: 	}
   56: }
   57: 
   58: func TestNoForbiddenPublicFields(t *testing.T) {
   59: 	forbidden := map[string]struct{}{
   60: 		"RawBody": {}, "ResponseBody": {}, "RequestBody": {}, "Authorization": {},
   61: 		"ResellerAPIKey": {}, "APIKeyEnv": {}, "BillingJWT": {}, "ServiceToken": {},
   62: 	}
   63: 	for _, typ := range []reflect.Type{reflect.TypeOf(Classification{}), reflect.TypeOf(Failure{})} {
   64: 		for i := 0; i < typ.NumField(); i++ {
   65: 			field := typ.Field(i)
   66: 			if _, found := forbidden[field.Name]; found {
   67: 				t.Fatalf("%s has forbidden field %s", typ.Name(), field.Name)
   68: 			}
   69: 		}
   70: 	}
   71: }

FILE: internal/application/ledger/exposure.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 1a173a5592272ab3711125a538d4d868960ffd44
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package ledger
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"fmt"
    6: 	"math"
    7: 
    8: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
    9: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
   10: )
   11: 
   12: type Exposure struct {
   13: 	Currency string
   14: 
   15: 	PendingAmountCents int64
   16: 	HasUnresolvedUsage bool
   17: }
   18: 
   19: type BalanceInput struct {
   20: 	RemoteBalanceCents   int64
   21: 	RequiredReserveCents int64
   22: 
   23: 	Exposure Exposure
   24: }
   25: 
   26: type BalanceResult struct {
   27: 	RemoteBalanceCents    int64
   28: 	PendingAmountCents    int64
   29: 	EffectiveBalanceCents int64
   30: 	RequiredReserveCents  int64
   31: 
   32: 	Allowed bool
   33: }
   34: 
   35: func (s *Service) LoadExposure(ctx context.Context, userID string, currency string) (Exposure, error) {
   36: 	if isBlank(userID) || currency != currencyRUB {
   37: 		return Exposure{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: exposure input", ErrInvalidLedgerInput)
   38: 	}
   39: 	snapshot, err := s.ledger.LoadExposure(ctx, userID, currency)
   40: 	if err != nil {
   41: 		return Exposure{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: load exposure: %w", ErrUsageStoreUnavailable, err)
   42: 	}
   43: 	return CalculateExposure(snapshot)
   44: }
   45: 
   46: func PendingAmountForRecord(record domain.UsageRecord) (int64, error) {
   47: 	switch record.Status {
   48: 	case domain.UsageStatusReserved:
   49: 		if record.EstimatedClientAmountCents < 0 {
   50: 			return 0, ErrRecordCorrupt
   51: 		}
   52: 		return record.EstimatedClientAmountCents, nil
   53: 	case domain.UsageStatusBillable, domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged:
   54: 		if record.RemainingAmountCents < 0 {
   55: 			return 0, ErrRecordCorrupt
   56: 		}
   57: 		return record.RemainingAmountCents, nil
   58: 	case domain.UsageStatusReleased, domain.UsageStatusCharged, domain.UsageStatusFailed:
   59: 		return 0, nil
   60: 	case domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed:
   61: 		return 0, ErrUnresolvedUsage
   62: 	default:
   63: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("%w: %s", ErrRecordCorrupt, record.Status)
   64: 	}
   65: }
   66: 
   67: func CalculateExposure(snapshot ports.UsageExposureSnapshot) (Exposure, error) {
   68: 	if snapshot.Currency != currencyRUB {
   69: 		return Exposure{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: currency", ErrInvalidLedgerInput)
   70: 	}
   71: 	if snapshot.ReservedEstimatedAmountCents < 0 || snapshot.BillableRemainingAmountCents < 0 || snapshot.PartiallyChargedRemainingAmountCents < 0 || snapshot.PricingFailedCount < 0 {
   72: 		return Exposure{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: negative exposure", ErrInvalidLedgerInput)
   73: 	}
   74: 	total, err := checkedAdd(snapshot.ReservedEstimatedAmountCents, snapshot.BillableRemainingAmountCents)
   75: 	if err != nil {
   76: 		return Exposure{}, err
   77: 	}
   78: 	total, err = checkedAdd(total, snapshot.PartiallyChargedRemainingAmountCents)
   79: 	if err != nil {
   80: 		return Exposure{}, err
   81: 	}
   82: 	return Exposure{Currency: snapshot.Currency, PendingAmountCents: total, HasUnresolvedUsage: snapshot.PricingFailedCount > 0}, nil
   83: }
   84: 
   85: func EvaluateBalance(input BalanceInput) (BalanceResult, error) {
   86: 	result := BalanceResult{
   87: 		RemoteBalanceCents:   input.RemoteBalanceCents,
   88: 		PendingAmountCents:   input.Exposure.PendingAmountCents,
   89: 		RequiredReserveCents: input.RequiredReserveCents,
   90: 	}
   91: 	if input.RemoteBalanceCents < 0 || input.RequiredReserveCents < 0 || input.Exposure.PendingAmountCents < 0 {
   92: 		return result, fmt.Errorf("%w: negative balance input", ErrInvalidLedgerInput)
   93: 	}
   94: 	if input.Exposure.Currency != currencyRUB {
   95: 		return result, fmt.Errorf("%w: currency", ErrInvalidLedgerInput)
   96: 	}
   97: 	if input.Exposure.HasUnresolvedUsage {
   98: 		return result, ErrUnresolvedUsage
   99: 	}
  100: 	effective, err := checkedSub(input.RemoteBalanceCents, input.Exposure.PendingAmountCents)
  101: 	if err != nil {
  102: 		return result, err
  103: 	}
  104: 	result.EffectiveBalanceCents = effective
  105: 	result.Allowed = effective >= input.RequiredReserveCents
  106: 	if !result.Allowed {
  107: 		return result, ErrInsufficientFunds
  108: 	}
  109: 	return result, nil
  110: }
  111: 
  112: func checkedAdd(left int64, right int64) (int64, error) {
  113: 	if right > 0 && left > math.MaxInt64-right {
  114: 		return 0, ErrAmountOverflow
  115: 	}
  116: 	return left + right, nil
  117: }
  118: 
  119: func checkedSub(left int64, right int64) (int64, error) {
  120: 	if right > 0 && left < math.MinInt64+right {
  121: 		return 0, ErrAmountOverflow
  122: 	}
  123: 	return left - right, nil
  124: }

FILE: internal/application/ledger/ledger_test.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 31906366489573f71e96853ffb17e99a9f3aff07
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package ledger
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"errors"

    7: 	"reflect"
    8: 	"strings"
    9: 	"sync"
   10: 	"testing"

   12: 
   13: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/application/pricing"
   14: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"

   16: )
   17: 
   18: type fixedClock struct {
   19: 	mu    sync.Mutex
   20: 	now   time.Time
   21: 	calls int
   22: }
   23: 
   24: func (c *fixedClock) Now() time.Time {
   25: 	c.mu.Lock()
   26: 	defer c.mu.Unlock()
   27: 	c.calls++
   28: 	return c.now
   29: }
   30: 
   31: func (c *fixedClock) set(now time.Time) { c.mu.Lock(); c.now = now; c.mu.Unlock() }
   32: func (c *fixedClock) count() int        { c.mu.Lock(); defer c.mu.Unlock(); return c.calls }
   33: 
   34: type fakeLedger struct {
   35: 	mu             sync.Mutex
   36: 	records        map[string]domain.UsageRecord
   37: 	casCalls       int
   38: 	forceCASResult *ports.UsageTransitionResult
   39: }
   40: 
   41: func newFakeLedger() *fakeLedger { return &fakeLedger{records: map[string]domain.UsageRecord{}} }
   42: 
   43: func (f *fakeLedger) CreateReserved(ctx context.Context, record domain.UsageRecord) (ports.UsageReserveResult, error) {
   44: 	f.mu.Lock()
   45: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   46: 	for _, existing := range f.records {
   47: 		if existing.UserID == record.UserID && existing.Status == domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed {
   48: 			return ports.UsageReserveResult{Outcome: ports.UsageReserveOutcomeUnresolvedUsage}, nil
   49: 		}
   50: 	}
   51: 	if existing, ok := f.records[record.LocalRequestID]; ok {
   52: 		copyValue := copyRecord(existing)
   53: 		return ports.UsageReserveResult{Outcome: ports.UsageReserveOutcomeLocalRequestExists, Existing: &copyValue}, nil
   54: 	}
   55: 	if record.IdempotencyKey != "" {
   56: 		for _, existing := range f.records {
   57: 			if existing.UserID == record.UserID && existing.EndpointKind == record.EndpointKind && existing.IdempotencyKey == record.IdempotencyKey {
   58: 				copyValue := copyRecord(existing)
   59: 				return ports.UsageReserveResult{Outcome: ports.UsageReserveOutcomeIdempotencyExists, Existing: &copyValue}, nil
   60: 			}
   61: 		}
   62: 	}
   63: 	f.records[record.LocalRequestID] = copyRecord(record)
   64: 	return ports.UsageReserveResult{Outcome: ports.UsageReserveOutcomeCreated}, nil
   65: }
   66: 
   67: func (f *fakeLedger) FindByLocalRequestID(ctx context.Context, localRequestID string) (*domain.UsageRecord, error) {
   68: 	f.mu.Lock()
   69: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   70: 	record, ok := f.records[localRequestID]
   71: 	if !ok {
   72: 		return nil, ports.ErrNotFound
   73: 	}
   74: 	copyValue := copyRecord(record)
   75: 	return &copyValue, nil
   76: }
   77: 
   78: func (f *fakeLedger) CompareAndSwap(ctx context.Context, localRequestID string, expectedStatus domain.UsageStatus, next domain.UsageRecord) (ports.UsageTransitionResult, error) {
   79: 	f.mu.Lock()
   80: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
   81: 	f.casCalls++
   82: 	if f.forceCASResult != nil {
   83: 		return *f.forceCASResult, nil
   84: 	}
   85: 	current, ok := f.records[localRequestID]
   86: 	if !ok {
   87: 		return ports.UsageTransitionResult{}, ports.ErrNotFound
   88: 	}
   89: 	if current.Status != expectedStatus {
   90: 		copyValue := copyRecord(current)
   91: 		return ports.UsageTransitionResult{Applied: false, Current: &copyValue}, nil
   92: 	}
   93: 	f.records[localRequestID] = copyRecord(next)
   94: 	return ports.UsageTransitionResult{Applied: true}, nil
   95: }
   96: 
   97: func (f *fakeLedger) LoadExposure(ctx context.Context, userID string, currency string) (ports.UsageExposureSnapshot, error) {
   98: 	f.mu.Lock()
   99: 	defer f.mu.Unlock()
  100: 	s := ports.UsageExposureSnapshot{Currency: currency}
  101: 	for _, r := range f.records {
  102: 		if r.UserID != userID || r.Currency != currency {
  103: 			continue
  104: 		}
  105: 		switch r.Status {
  106: 		case domain.UsageStatusReserved:
  107: 			s.ReservedEstimatedAmountCents += r.EstimatedClientAmountCents
  108: 		case domain.UsageStatusBillable:
  109: 			s.BillableRemainingAmountCents += r.RemainingAmountCents
  110: 		case domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged:
  111: 			s.PartiallyChargedRemainingAmountCents += r.RemainingAmountCents
  112: 		case domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed:
  113: 			s.PricingFailedCount++
  114: 		}
  115: 	}
  116: 	return s, nil
  117: }
  118:

  120: 	return nil, nil
  121: }
  122:

  124: 	return nil, nil

  126: 
  127: func (f *fakeLedger) PrepareChargeBatch(ctx context.Context, plan ports.UsageChargeBatchPlan) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {

  129: }

  139: func (f *fakeLedger) put(record domain.UsageRecord) {
  140: 	f.mu.Lock()
  141: 	f.records[record.LocalRequestID] = copyRecord(record)
  142: 	f.mu.Unlock()
  143: }
  144: func (f *fakeLedger) len() int      { f.mu.Lock(); defer f.mu.Unlock(); return len(f.records) }
  145: func (f *fakeLedger) casCount() int { f.mu.Lock(); defer f.mu.Unlock(); return f.casCalls }

  147: func newServiceForTest(t *testing.T) (*Service, *fakeLedger, *fixedClock) {
  148: 	t.Helper()
  149: 	store := newFakeLedger()
  150: 	clock := &fixedClock{now: time.Date(2026, 6, 12, 9, 0, 0, 0, time.FixedZone("MSK", 3*60*60))}
  151: 	svc, err := NewService(store, clock)
  152: 	if err != nil {
  153: 		t.Fatal(err)
  154: 	}
  155: 	return svc, store, clock
  156: }

  158: func validReserveInput(id string) ReserveInput {
  159: 	return ReserveInput{LocalRequestID: id, UserID: "user_1", APIKeyID: "key_1", APIFamily: domain.APIFamilyOpenAICompatible, EndpointKind: domain.EndpointChat, ClientModel: "gpt-4o", BillingModel: string(domain.ProviderOpenAI) + ":" + "gpt-4o", SelectedRouteID: "route_1", SelectedResellerID: "reseller_1", ProviderType: domain.ProviderOpenAI, ProviderModel: "gpt-4o", EstimatedUsage: domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 10, OutputTokens: 2}, EstimatedClientAmountCents: 50, EstimatedUpstreamCostCents: 30, Currency: "RUB"}
  160: }
  161: 
  162: func reserveRecord(id string) domain.UsageRecord {
  163: 	now := time.Date(2026, 6, 12, 6, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
  164: 	input := validReserveInput(id)
  165: 	return domain.UsageRecord{LocalRequestID: input.LocalRequestID, UserID: input.UserID, APIKeyID: input.APIKeyID, APIFamily: input.APIFamily, EndpointKind: input.EndpointKind, ClientModel: input.ClientModel, BillingModel: string(input.ProviderType) + ":" + input.ClientModel, SelectedRouteID: input.SelectedRouteID, SelectedResellerID: input.SelectedResellerID, ProviderType: input.ProviderType, ProviderModel: input.ProviderModel, EstimatedUsage: input.EstimatedUsage, EstimatedClientAmountCents: input.EstimatedClientAmountCents, EstimatedUpstreamCostCents: input.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents, Currency: "RUB", UsageCompleteness: string(pricing.UsageCompletenessMissing), Status: domain.UsageStatusReserved, CreatedAt: now, ReservedAt: &now, UpdatedAt: now}
  166: }
  167: 
  168: func billableRecord(id string) domain.UsageRecord {
  169: 	r := reserveRecord(id)
  170: 	now := time.Date(2026, 6, 12, 6, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
  171: 	r.Status = domain.UsageStatusBillable
  172: 	r.Usage = domain.TokenUsage{InputTokens: 1}
  173: 	r.UsageCompleteness = string(pricing.UsageCompletenessDetailed)
  174: 	r.ClientAmountCents = 10
  175: 	r.RemainingAmountCents = 10
  176: 	r.BillableAt = &now
  177: 	return r
  178: }
  179: 
  180: func chargedRecord(id string) domain.UsageRecord {
  181: 	r := billableRecord(id)
  182: 	now := time.Date(2026, 6, 12, 6, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
  183: 	r.Status = domain.UsageStatusCharged
  184: 	r.ChargedAmountCents = r.ClientAmountCents
  185: 	r.RemainingAmountCents = 0
  186: 	r.ChargedAt = &now
  187: 	r.BillingChargeRequestID = "billchg_" + strings.Repeat("a", 64)
  188: 	return r
  189: }
  190: 
  191: func failedRecord(id string) domain.UsageRecord {
  192: 	r := reserveRecord(id)
  193: 	now := time.Date(2026, 6, 12, 6, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
  194: 	r.Status = domain.UsageStatusFailed
  195: 	r.FailureReason = "internal_failure"
  196: 	r.FailedAt = &now
  197: 	return r
  198: }
  199: 
  200: func TestStateMachineAllowedForbiddenAndUnknown(t *testing.T) {
  201: 	allowed := [][2]domain.UsageStatus{{domain.UsageStatusReserved, domain.UsageStatusReleased}, {domain.UsageStatusReserved, domain.UsageStatusBillable}, {domain.UsageStatusReserved, domain.UsageStatusFailed}, {domain.UsageStatusReserved, domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed}, {domain.UsageStatusBillable, domain.UsageStatusCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusBillable, domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusBillable, domain.UsageStatusFailed}, {domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged, domain.UsageStatusCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged, domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged, domain.UsageStatusFailed}, {domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed, domain.UsageStatusBillable}, {domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed, domain.UsageStatusCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed, domain.UsageStatusFailed}}
  202: 	for _, pair := range allowed {
  203: 		if !CanTransition(pair[0], pair[1]) || ValidateTransition(pair[0], pair[1]) != nil {
  204: 			t.Fatalf("allowed transition rejected: %s -> %s", pair[0], pair[1])
  205: 		}
  206: 	}
  207: 	forbidden := [][2]domain.UsageStatus{{domain.UsageStatusReleased, domain.UsageStatusBillable}, {domain.UsageStatusReleased, domain.UsageStatusReserved}, {domain.UsageStatusCharged, domain.UsageStatusBillable}, {domain.UsageStatusCharged, domain.UsageStatusReserved}, {domain.UsageStatusFailed, domain.UsageStatusCharged}, {domain.UsageStatusFailed, domain.UsageStatusReserved}, {domain.UsageStatusBillable, domain.UsageStatusReserved}, {domain.UsageStatusPricingFailed, domain.UsageStatusReserved}}
  208: 	for _, pair := range forbidden {
  209: 		if CanTransition(pair[0], pair[1]) || !errors.Is(ValidateTransition(pair[0], pair[1]), ErrInvalidStateTransition) {
  210: 			t.Fatalf("forbidden transition accepted: %s -> %s", pair[0], pair[1])
  211: 		}
  212: 	}
  213: 	if !errors.Is(ValidateTransition(domain.UsageStatus("weird"), domain.UsageStatusReserved), ErrInvalidUsageStatus) {
  214: 		t.Fatal("unknown from not invalid")
  215: 	}
  216: 	if !errors.Is(ValidateTransition(domain.UsageStatusReserved, domain.UsageStatus("weird")), ErrInvalidUsageStatus) {
  217: 		t.Fatal("unknown to not invalid")
  218: 	}
  219: }
  220: 
  221: func TestReserveValidationAndIdempotencyKey(t *testing.T) {
  222: 	tests := []struct {
  223: 		name   string
  224: 		mutate func(*ReserveInput)
  225: 		want   error
  226: 	}{
  227: 		{"valid reserve created", func(*ReserveInput) {}, nil}, {"local request without llmreq_ rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.LocalRequestID = "x" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"llmreq_ without suffix rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.LocalRequestID = "llmreq_" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank user ID rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.UserID = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank API key ID rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.APIKeyID = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"empty API family rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.APIFamily = "" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"empty endpoint kind rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EndpointKind = "" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank client model rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.ClientModel = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank billing model rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.BillingModel = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank route ID rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.SelectedRouteID = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank reseller ID rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.SelectedResellerID = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"empty provider type rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.ProviderType = "" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"blank provider model rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.ProviderModel = " " }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"negative estimated client amount rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EstimatedClientAmountCents = -1 }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"negative estimated upstream cost rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents = -1 }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"negative estimated usage rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EstimatedUsage.InputTokens = -1 }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"currency other than RUB rejected", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.Currency = "USD" }, ErrInvalidLedgerInput}, {"zero estimated amount accepted", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EstimatedClientAmountCents = 0 }, nil}, {"zero estimated usage accepted", func(i *ReserveInput) { i.EstimatedUsage = domain.TokenUsage{} }, nil},
  228: 	}
  229: 	for n, tc := range tests {
  230: 		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
  231: 			svc, _, _ := newServiceForTest(t)
  232: 			input := validReserveInput("llmreq_val_" + string(rune('a'+n)))
  233: 			tc.mutate(&input)
  234: 			res, err := svc.Reserve(t.Context(), input)
  235: 			if tc.want != nil && !errors.Is(err, tc.want) {
  236: 				t.Fatalf("err=%v want %v", err, tc.want)
  237: 			}
  238: 			if tc.want == nil {
  239: 				if err != nil {
  240: 					t.Fatal(err)
  241: 				}
  242: 				if res.Disposition != ReserveDispositionCreated {
  243: 					t.Fatal(res.Disposition)
  244: 				}
  245: 			}
  246: 		})
  247: 	}
  248: 
  249: 	for _, tc := range []struct {
  250: 		name    string
  251: 		key     *string
  252: 		wantErr bool
  253: 		want    string
  254: 	}{{"nil key accepted as absent", nil, false, ""}, {"exact key preserved", ptr("Key-1"), false, "Key-1"}, {"empty provided key rejected", ptr(""), true, ""}, {"whitespace-only provided key rejected", ptr("  \t"), true, ""}, {"leading/trailing spaces in nonblank key preserved", ptr(" key "), false, " key "}, {"different case preserved", ptr("AbC"), false, "AbC"}, {"key not hashed", ptr("plain-key"), false, "plain-key"}} {
  255: 		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
  256: 			svc, _, _ := newServiceForTest(t)
  257: 			input := validReserveInput("llmreq_key_" + strings.ReplaceAll(tc.name, " ", "_"))
  258: 			input.IdempotencyKey = tc.key
  259: 			res, err := svc.Reserve(t.Context(), input)
  260: 			if tc.wantErr {
  261: 				if !errors.Is(err, ErrInvalidLedgerInput) {
  262: 					t.Fatalf("err=%v", err)
  263: 				}
  264: 				return
  265: 			}
  266: 			if err != nil {
  267: 				t.Fatal(err)
  268: 			}
  269: 			if res.Record.IdempotencyKey != tc.want {
  270: 				t.Fatalf("key=%q want %q", res.Record.IdempotencyKey, tc.want)
  271: 			}
  272: 		})
  273: 	}
  274: }
