  343: 
  344: func encodeExpectedRecord(
  345: 	value domain.UsageRecord,
  346: ) ([]byte, error) {
  347: 	body, err := json.Marshal(canonicalUsageRecord(value))
  348: 	if err != nil {
  349: 		return nil, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  350: 	}
  351: 	if _, err := decodeExpectedRecord(body); err != nil {
  352: 		return nil, err
  353: 	}
  354: 	return body, nil
  355: }
  356: 
  357: func decodeExpectedRecord(raw []byte) (domain.UsageRecord, error) {
  358: 	trimmed := bytes.TrimSpace(raw)
  359: 	if len(trimmed) < 2 ||
  360: 		trimmed[0] != '{' ||
  361: 		trimmed[len(trimmed)-1] != '}' {
  362: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  363: 	}
  364: 
  365: 	var dto usageRecordJSON
  366: 	decoder := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(trimmed))
  367: 	decoder.DisallowUnknownFields()
  368: 	if err := decoder.Decode(&dto); err != nil {
  369: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  370: 	}
  371: 	if err := decoder.Decode(&struct{}{}); !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
  372: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  373: 	}
  374: 	return dto.domainRecord()
  375: }
  376: 
  377: func (dto usageRecordJSON) domainRecord() (domain.UsageRecord, error) {
  378: 	required := []bool{
  379: 		dto.LocalRequestID != nil,
  380: 		dto.UserID != nil,
  381: 		dto.APIKeyID != nil,
  382: 		dto.APIFamily != nil,
  383: 		dto.EndpointKind != nil,
  384: 		dto.ClientModel != nil,
  385: 		dto.BillingModel != nil,
  386: 		dto.SelectedRouteID != nil,
  387: 		dto.SelectedResellerID != nil,
  388: 		dto.ProviderType != nil,
  389: 		dto.ProviderModel != nil,
  390: 		dto.EstimatedUsage != nil,
  391: 		dto.Usage != nil,
  392: 		dto.EstimatedClientAmountCents != nil,
  393: 		dto.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents != nil,
  394: 		dto.ClientAmountCents != nil,
  395: 		dto.ChargedAmountCents != nil,
  396: 		dto.RemainingAmountCents != nil,
  397: 		dto.ActualUpstreamCostCents != nil,
  398: 		dto.Currency != nil,
  399: 		dto.UsageCompleteness != nil,
  400: 		dto.Status != nil,
  401: 		dto.BillingChargeRequestID != nil,
  402: 		dto.CreatedAt != nil,
  403: 		dto.UpdatedAt != nil,
  404: 	}
  405: 	for _, present := range required {
  406: 		if !present {
  407: 			return domain.UsageRecord{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  408: 		}
  409: 	}
  410: 
  411: 	estimated, err := dto.EstimatedUsage.domainUsage()
  412: 	if err != nil {
  413: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, err
  414: 	}
  415: 	actual, err := dto.Usage.domainUsage()
  416: 	if err != nil {
  417: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, err
  418: 	}
  419: 
  420: 	value := domain.UsageRecord{
  421: 		LocalRequestID:             *dto.LocalRequestID,
  422: 		UserID:                     *dto.UserID,
  423: 		APIKeyID:                   *dto.APIKeyID,
  424: 		APIFamily:                  *dto.APIFamily,
  425: 		EndpointKind:               *dto.EndpointKind,
  426: 		ClientModel:                *dto.ClientModel,
  427: 		BillingModel:               *dto.BillingModel,
  428: 		SelectedRouteID:            *dto.SelectedRouteID,
  429: 		SelectedResellerID:         *dto.SelectedResellerID,
  430: 		ProviderType:               *dto.ProviderType,
  431: 		ProviderModel:              *dto.ProviderModel,
  432: 		EstimatedUsage:             estimated,
  433: 		Usage:                      actual,
  434: 		EstimatedClientAmountCents: *dto.EstimatedClientAmountCents,
  435: 		EstimatedUpstreamCostCents: *dto.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents,
  436: 		ClientAmountCents:          *dto.ClientAmountCents,
  437: 		ChargedAmountCents:         *dto.ChargedAmountCents,
  438: 		RemainingAmountCents:       *dto.RemainingAmountCents,
  439: 		ActualUpstreamCostCents:    *dto.ActualUpstreamCostCents,
  440: 		Currency:                   *dto.Currency,
  441: 		UsageCompleteness:          *dto.UsageCompleteness,
  442: 		Status:                     *dto.Status,
  443: 		BillingChargeRequestID:     *dto.BillingChargeRequestID,
  444: 		CreatedAt:                  canonicalTime(*dto.CreatedAt),
  445: 		ReservedAt:                 cloneCanonicalTime(dto.ReservedAt),
  446: 		ReleasedAt:                 cloneCanonicalTime(dto.ReleasedAt),
  447: 		BillableAt:                 cloneCanonicalTime(dto.BillableAt),
  448: 		ChargedAt:                  cloneCanonicalTime(dto.ChargedAt),
  449: 		FailedAt:                   cloneCanonicalTime(dto.FailedAt),
  450: 		UpdatedAt:                  canonicalTime(*dto.UpdatedAt),
  451: 	}
  452: 	if dto.IdempotencyKey != nil {
  453: 		value.IdempotencyKey = *dto.IdempotencyKey
  454: 	}
  455: 	if dto.ProviderRequestID != nil {
  456: 		value.ProviderRequestID = *dto.ProviderRequestID
  457: 	}
  458: 	if dto.ProviderResponseModel != nil {
  459: 		value.ProviderResponseModel = *dto.ProviderResponseModel
  460: 	}
  461: 	if dto.FailureReason != nil {
  462: 		value.FailureReason = *dto.FailureReason
  463: 	}
  464: 
  465: 	if err := validateExpectedRecordPersistence(value); err != nil {
  466: 		return domain.UsageRecord{}, err
  467: 	}
  468: 	return value, nil
  469: }
  470: 
  471: func (dto tokenUsageJSON) domainUsage() (domain.TokenUsage, error) {
  472: 	required := []bool{
  473: 		dto.InputTokens != nil,
  474: 		dto.CachedInputTokens != nil,
  475: 		dto.OutputTokens != nil,
  476: 		dto.ReasoningTokens != nil,
  477: 		dto.ImageInputTokens != nil,
  478: 		dto.AudioInputTokens != nil,
  479: 		dto.AudioOutputTokens != nil,
  480: 		dto.FileInputTokens != nil,
  481: 		dto.VideoInputTokens != nil,
  482: 		dto.ImageGenerationUnits != nil,
  483: 	}
  484: 	for _, present := range required {
  485: 		if !present {
  486: 			return domain.TokenUsage{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  487: 		}
  488: 	}
  489: 
  490: 	value := domain.TokenUsage{
  491: 		InputTokens:          *dto.InputTokens,
  492: 		CachedInputTokens:    *dto.CachedInputTokens,
  493: 		OutputTokens:         *dto.OutputTokens,
  494: 		ReasoningTokens:      *dto.ReasoningTokens,
  495: 		ImageInputTokens:     *dto.ImageInputTokens,
  496: 		AudioInputTokens:     *dto.AudioInputTokens,
  497: 		AudioOutputTokens:    *dto.AudioOutputTokens,
  498: 		FileInputTokens:      *dto.FileInputTokens,
  499: 		VideoInputTokens:     *dto.VideoInputTokens,
  500: 		ImageGenerationUnits: *dto.ImageGenerationUnits,
  501: 	}
  502: 	if !nonNegativeUsage(value) {
  503: 		return domain.TokenUsage{}, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  504: 	}
  505: 	return value, nil
  506: }
  507: 
  508: func validateExpectedRecordPersistence(
  509: 	value domain.UsageRecord,
  510: ) error {
  511: 	if value.LocalRequestID == "" ||
  512: 		value.UserID == "" ||
  513: 		value.APIKeyID == "" ||
  514: 		!validChargeAPIFamily(value.APIFamily) ||
  515: 		!validChargeEndpointKind(value.EndpointKind) ||
  516: 		value.ClientModel == "" ||
  517: 		value.BillingModel == "" ||
  518: 		value.SelectedRouteID == "" ||
  519: 		value.SelectedResellerID == "" ||
  520: 		!validChargeProviderType(value.ProviderType) ||
  521: 		value.ProviderModel == "" ||
  522: 		value.Currency != "RUB" ||
  523: 		!validUsageCompleteness(value.UsageCompleteness) ||
  524: 		value.BillingChargeRequestID == "" ||
  525: 		value.CreatedAt.IsZero() ||
  526: 		value.UpdatedAt.IsZero() ||
  527: 		!nonNegativeUsage(value.EstimatedUsage) ||
  528: 		!nonNegativeUsage(value.Usage) ||
  529: 		value.EstimatedClientAmountCents < 0 ||
  530: 		value.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents < 0 ||
  531: 		value.ClientAmountCents <= 0 ||
  532: 		value.ChargedAmountCents < 0 ||
  533: 		value.RemainingAmountCents <= 0 ||
  534: 		value.ChargedAmountCents > value.ClientAmountCents ||
  535: 		value.RemainingAmountCents !=
  536: 			value.ClientAmountCents-value.ChargedAmountCents ||
  537: 		value.ActualUpstreamCostCents < 0 {
  538: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  539: 	}
  540: 
  541: 	switch value.Status {
  542: 	case
  543: 		domain.UsageStatusBillable,
  544: 		domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged:
  545: 	default:
  546: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  547: 	}
  548: 	return nil
  549: }
  550: 
  551: func validChargeProviderType(value domain.ProviderType) bool {
  552: 	switch value {
  553: 	case
  554: 		domain.ProviderOpenAI,
  555: 		domain.ProviderOpenRouter,
  556: 		domain.ProviderTogether,
  557: 		domain.ProviderGroq,
  558: 		domain.ProviderOllama,
  559: 		domain.ProviderLMStudio,
  560: 		domain.ProviderVLLM,
  561: 		domain.ProviderGemini,
  562: 		domain.ProviderAnthropic,
  563: 		domain.ProviderHydra:
  564: 		return true
  565: 	default:
  566: 		return false
  567: 	}
  568: }
  569: 
  570: func validChargeAPIFamily(value domain.APIFamily) bool {
  571: 	switch value {
  572: 	case
  573: 		domain.APIFamilyOpenAICompatible,
  574: 		domain.APIFamilyGeminiNative,
  575: 		domain.APIFamilyAnthropicNative,
  576: 		domain.APIFamilyOllamaNative:
  577: 		return true
  578: 	default:
  579: 		return false
  580: 	}
  581: }
  582: 
  583: func validChargeEndpointKind(value domain.EndpointKind) bool {
  584: 	switch value {
  585: 	case
  586: 		domain.EndpointChat,
  587: 		domain.EndpointEmbeddings,
  588: 		domain.EndpointImagesGeneration:
  589: 		return true
  590: 	default:
  591: 		return false
  592: 	}
  593: }
  594: 
  595: func validUsageCompleteness(value string) bool {
  596: 	switch value {
  597: 	case "detailed", "aggregate", "estimated", "missing", "failed":
  598: 		return true
  599: 	default:
  600: 		return false
  601: 	}
  602: }
  603: 
  604: func nullableInt64(value *int64) any {
  605: 	if value == nil {
  606: 		return nil
  607: 	}
  608: 	return *value
  609: }
  610: 
  611: func nonNegativeUsage(value domain.TokenUsage) bool {
  612: 	return value.InputTokens >= 0 &&
  613: 		value.CachedInputTokens >= 0 &&
  614: 		value.OutputTokens >= 0 &&
  615: 		value.ReasoningTokens >= 0 &&
  616: 		value.ImageInputTokens >= 0 &&
  617: 		value.AudioInputTokens >= 0 &&
  618: 		value.AudioOutputTokens >= 0 &&
  619: 		value.FileInputTokens >= 0 &&
  620: 		value.VideoInputTokens >= 0 &&
  621: 		value.ImageGenerationUnits >= 0
  622: }

   14: func TestExpectedRecordJSONStrictRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
   15: 	value := testExpectedRecord()
   16: 
   17: 	body, err := encodeExpectedRecord(value)
   18: 	if err != nil {
   19: 		t.Fatalf("encodeExpectedRecord: %v", err)
   20: 	}
   21: 	got, err := decodeExpectedRecord(body)
   22: 	if err != nil {
   23: 		t.Fatalf("decodeExpectedRecord: %v", err)
   24: 	}
   25: 	if !sameUsageRecord(got, value) {
   26: 		t.Fatalf("round trip mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", got, value)
   27: 	}
   28: }

   30: func TestExpectedRecordJSONRejectsUnknownField(t *testing.T) {
   31: 	body, err := encodeExpectedRecord(testExpectedRecord())
   32: 	if err != nil {
   33: 		t.Fatal(err)
   34: 	}
   35: 
   36: 	var object map[string]json.RawMessage
   37: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &object); err != nil {
   38: 		t.Fatal(err)
   39: 	}
   40: 	object["unknown"] = json.RawMessage(`true`)
   41: 	body, err = json.Marshal(object)
   42: 	if err != nil {
   43: 		t.Fatal(err)
   44: 	}
   45: 
   46: 	_, err = decodeExpectedRecord(body)
   47: 	if !errors.Is(err, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation) {
   48: 		t.Fatalf("error = %v, want contract violation", err)
   49: 	}
   50: }

   70: func TestSameBatchCommandIgnoresLifecycleAndTimestamps(t *testing.T) {
   71: 	left := testBatch()
   72: 	right := left
   73: 	right.Status = domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded
   74: 	balance := int64(100)
   75: 	right.BillingResponseBalanceCents = &balance
   76: 	chargedAt := left.CreatedAt.Add(time.Minute)
   77: 	right.ChargedAt = &chargedAt
   78: 	right.CreatedAt = left.CreatedAt.Add(time.Hour)
   79: 	right.UpdatedAt = right.CreatedAt
   80: 
   81: 	if !sameBatchCommand(left, right) {
   82: 		t.Fatal("lifecycle and timestamps must not change command identity")
   83: 	}
   84: 	right.AmountCents++
   85: 	if sameBatchCommand(left, right) {
   86: 		t.Fatal("amount must change command identity")
   87: 	}
   88: }

  196: func TestCanonicalUTCTimeRejectsNonUTC(t *testing.T) {
  197: 	if !isCanonicalUTCTime(time.Unix(1, 0).UTC()) {
  198: 		t.Fatal("UTC time must be accepted")
  199: 	}

  201: 		time.FixedZone("offset", 3*60*60),
  202: 	)) {
  203: 		t.Fatal("non-UTC time must be rejected")
  204: 	}

FILE: internal/infrastructure/postgres/usage_charge_prepare.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: b883db046287f9d9b2ea2797129d921ec0be537d

    1: package postgres
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"errors"
    6: 
    7: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
    8: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
    9: 	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
   10: )
   11: 
   12: const insertBillingChargeBatchSQL = `
   13: INSERT INTO tokenio_billing_charge_batches (
   14:     id,
   15:     user_id,
   16:     billing_subject_user_id,
   17:     provider_type,
   18:     client_model,
   19:     billing_model,
   20:     input_tokens,
   21:     output_tokens,
   22:     amount_cents,
   23:     currency,
   24:     billing_status,
   25:     billing_response_balance_cents,
   26:     billing_error_code,
   27:     created_at,
   28:     charged_at,
   29:     failed_at,
   30:     updated_at
   31: )
   32: VALUES (
   33:     $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8, $9,
   34:     $10, $11, $12, $13, $14, $15, $16, $17
   35: )
   36: `
   37: 
   38: const insertBillingChargeAllocationSQL = `
   39: INSERT INTO tokenio_billing_charge_allocations (
   40:     id,
   41:     batch_id,
   42:     local_request_id,
   43:     position,
   44:     charged_amount_cents,
   45:     remaining_amount_cents,
   46:     created_at
   47: )
   48: VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)
   49: `
   50: 
   51: const insertBillingChargeExpectedRecordSQL = `
   52: INSERT INTO tokenio_billing_charge_expected_records (
   53:     batch_id,
   54:     local_request_id,
   55:     position,
   56:     expected_record,
   57:     created_at
   58: )
   59: VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, $5)
   60: `
   61: 
   62: const lockUsageRecordsForChargeSQL = `
   63: SELECT
   64: ` + usageRecordColumns + `
   65: FROM tokenio_usage_records
   66: WHERE local_request_id = ANY($1::text[])
   67: ORDER BY local_request_id ASC
   68: FOR UPDATE
   69: `
   70: 
   71: const activeChargeClaimExistsSQL = `
   72: SELECT EXISTS (
   73:     SELECT 1
   74:     FROM tokenio_billing_charge_allocations AS allocation
   75:     JOIN tokenio_billing_charge_batches AS batch
   76:       ON batch.id = allocation.batch_id
   77:     WHERE allocation.local_request_id = ANY($1::text[])
   78:       AND batch.billing_status IN ('pending', 'failed')
   79: )
   80: `
   81: 
   82: func (r *UsageLedger) PrepareChargeBatch(
   83: 	ctx context.Context,
   84: 	plan ports.UsageChargeBatchPlan,
   85: ) (ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot, error) {
   86: 	if err := validateChargePlanPersistence(plan); err != nil {
   87: 		return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{}, err
   88: 	}
   89: 
   90: 	var result ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot
   91: 	err := InTx(
   92: 		ctx,
   93: 		r.db,
   94: 		pgx.TxOptions{IsoLevel: pgx.Serializable},
   95: 		func(tx pgx.Tx) error {
   96: 			if _, err := tx.Exec(
   97: 				ctx,
   98: 				`
   99: SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(
  100:     hashtextextended('tokenio_charge_batch:' || $1, 0)
  101: )
  102: `,
  103: 				plan.Batch.ID,
  104: 			); err != nil {
  105: 				return NormalizeError(err)
  106: 			}
  107: 
  108: 			existing, err := loadBillingChargeSnapshot(
  109: 				ctx,
  110: 				tx,
  111: 				plan.Batch.ID,
  112: 				true,
  113: 			)
  114: 			switch {
  115: 			case err == nil:
  116: 				if err := compareChargePlanReplay(
  117: 					plan,
  118: 					existing,
  119: 				); err != nil {
  120: 					return err
  121: 				}
  122: 				result = existing
  123: 				return nil
  124: 			case errors.Is(err, ports.ErrNotFound):
  125: 			default:
  126: 				return err
  127: 			}
  128: 
  129: 			currentByID, err := lockUsageRecordsForCharge(
  130: 				ctx,
  131: 				tx,
  132: 				plan.ExpectedRecords,
  133: 			)
  134: 			if err != nil {
  135: 				return err
  136: 			}
  137: 			for _, expected := range plan.ExpectedRecords {
  138: 				current, ok := currentByID[expected.LocalRequestID]
  139: 				if !ok || !sameUsageRecord(current, expected) {
  140: 					return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  141: 				}
  142: 			}
  143: 
  144: 			localRequestIDs := make([]string, 0, len(plan.ExpectedRecords))
  145: 			for _, expected := range plan.ExpectedRecords {
  146: 				localRequestIDs = append(
  147: 					localRequestIDs,
  148: 					expected.LocalRequestID,
  149: 				)
  150: 			}
  151: 			if err := verifyHistoricalClaims(
  152: 				ctx,
  153: 				tx,
  154: 				plan.ExpectedRecords,
  155: 			); err != nil {
  156: 				return err
  157: 			}
  158: 
  159: 			var activeClaim bool
  160: 			if err := tx.QueryRow(
  161: 				ctx,
  162: 				activeChargeClaimExistsSQL,
  163: 				localRequestIDs,
  164: 			).Scan(&activeClaim); err != nil {
  165: 				return normalizeRegistryReadError(err)
  166: 			}
  167: 			if activeClaim {
  168: 				return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  169: 			}
  170: 
  171: 			if err := insertChargeCommand(ctx, tx, plan); err != nil {
  172: 				return err
  173: 			}
  174: 
  175: 			for index, expected := range plan.ExpectedRecords {
  176: 				tag, err := tx.Exec(
  177: 					ctx,
  178: 					`
  179: UPDATE tokenio_usage_records
  180: SET billing_charge_request_id = $2
  181: WHERE local_request_id = $1
  182: `,
  183: 					expected.LocalRequestID,
  184: 					plan.Batch.ID,
  185: 				)
  186: 				if err != nil {
  187: 					return NormalizeError(err)
  188: 				}
  189: 				if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
  190: 					return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  191: 				}
  192: 
  193: 				postClaim := postClaimRecord(
  194: 					expected,
  195: 					plan.Batch.ID,
  196: 				)
  197: 				body, err := encodeExpectedRecord(postClaim)
  198: 				if err != nil {
  199: 					return err
  200: 				}
  201: 				if _, err := tx.Exec(
  202: 					ctx,
  203: 					insertBillingChargeExpectedRecordSQL,
  204: 					plan.Batch.ID,
  205: 					expected.LocalRequestID,
  206: 					index,
  207: 					body,
  208: 					plan.Batch.CreatedAt,
  209: 				); err != nil {
  210: 					return NormalizeError(err)
  211: 				}
  212: 			}
  213: 
  214: 			result, err = loadBillingChargeSnapshot(
  215: 				ctx,
  216: 				tx,
  217: 				plan.Batch.ID,
  218: 				false,
  219: 			)
  220: 			return err
  221: 		},
  222: 	)
  223: 	if err != nil {
  224: 		return ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot{}, err
  225: 	}
  226: 	return result, nil
  227: }
  228: 
  229: func validateChargePlanPersistence(
  230: 	plan ports.UsageChargeBatchPlan,
  231: ) error {
  232: 	batch := plan.Batch
  233: 	if batch.ID == "" ||
  234: 		batch.UserID == "" ||
  235: 		batch.BillingSubjectUserID == "" ||
  236: 		batch.ProviderType == "" ||
  237: 		batch.ClientModel == "" ||
  238: 		batch.BillingModel == "" ||
  239: 		batch.InputTokens < 0 ||
  240: 		batch.OutputTokens < 0 ||
  241: 		batch.AmountCents <= 0 ||
  242: 		batch.Currency != "RUB" ||
  243: 		batch.Status != domain.BillingChargeStatusPending ||
  244: 		batch.BillingResponseBalanceCents != nil ||
  245: 		batch.BillingErrorCode != "" ||
  246: 		batch.ChargedAt != nil ||
  247: 		batch.FailedAt != nil ||
  248: 		!isCanonicalUTCTime(batch.CreatedAt) ||
  249: 		!isCanonicalUTCTime(batch.UpdatedAt) ||
  250: 		!batch.CreatedAt.Equal(batch.UpdatedAt) {
  251: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  252: 	}
  253: 
  254: 	if len(plan.Allocations) == 0 ||
  255: 		len(plan.Allocations) != len(plan.ExpectedRecords) {
  256: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  257: 	}
  258: 
  259: 	seen := make(map[string]struct{}, len(plan.Allocations))
  260: 	for index := range plan.Allocations {
  261: 		allocation := plan.Allocations[index]
  262: 		expected := plan.ExpectedRecords[index]
  263: 
  264: 		if allocation.ID == "" ||
  265: 			allocation.BatchID != batch.ID ||
  266: 			allocation.LocalRequestID == "" ||
  267: 			allocation.LocalRequestID != expected.LocalRequestID ||
  268: 			allocation.ChargedAmountCents <= 0 ||
  269: 			allocation.RemainingAmountCents < 0 ||
  270: 			!isCanonicalUTCTime(allocation.CreatedAt) ||
  271: 			!allocation.CreatedAt.Equal(batch.CreatedAt) ||
  272: 			expected.LocalRequestID == "" ||
  273: 			expected.Status != domain.UsageStatusBillable &&
  274: 				expected.Status !=
  275: 					domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged ||
  276: 			expected.Status == domain.UsageStatusBillable &&
  277: 				expected.BillingChargeRequestID != "" ||
  278: 			expected.Status ==
  279: 				domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged &&
  280: 				expected.BillingChargeRequestID == "" ||
  281: 			expected.RemainingAmountCents <= 0 {
  282: 			return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  283: 		}
  284: 		if _, exists := seen[expected.LocalRequestID]; exists {
  285: 			return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  286: 		}
  287: 		seen[expected.LocalRequestID] = struct{}{}
  288: 
  289: 		postClaim := postClaimRecord(expected, batch.ID)
  290: 		if err := validateExpectedRecordPersistence(postClaim); err != nil {
  291: 			return err
  292: 		}
  293: 	}
  294: 	return nil
  295: }
  296: 
  297: func lockUsageRecordsForCharge(
  298: 	ctx context.Context,
  299: 	tx pgx.Tx,
  300: 	expected []domain.UsageRecord,
  301: ) (map[string]domain.UsageRecord, error) {
  302: 	localRequestIDs := make([]string, 0, len(expected))
  303: 	for _, record := range expected {
  304: 		localRequestIDs = append(localRequestIDs, record.LocalRequestID)
  305: 	}
  306: 
  307: 	rows, err := tx.Query(
  308: 		ctx,
  309: 		lockUsageRecordsForChargeSQL,
  310: 		localRequestIDs,
  311: 	)
  312: 	if err != nil {
  313: 		return nil, normalizeRegistryReadError(err)
  314: 	}
  315: 	defer rows.Close()
  316: 
  317: 	result := make(map[string]domain.UsageRecord, len(expected))
  318: 	for rows.Next() {
  319: 		record, err := scanUsageRecord(rows)
  320: 		if err != nil {
  321: 			return nil, err
  322: 		}
  323: 		if _, exists := result[record.LocalRequestID]; exists {
  324: 			return nil, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  325: 		}
  326: 		result[record.LocalRequestID] = record
  327: 	}
  328: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
  329: 		return nil, normalizeRegistryReadError(err)
  330: 	}
  331: 	if len(result) != len(expected) {
  332: 		return nil, ports.ErrStoreConflict
  333: 	}
  334: 	return result, nil
  335: }
  336: 
  337: func verifyHistoricalClaims(
  338: 	ctx context.Context,
  339: 	tx pgx.Tx,
  340: 	expected []domain.UsageRecord,
  341: ) error {
  342: 	for _, record := range expected {
  343: 		if record.BillingChargeRequestID == "" {
  344: 			continue
  345: 		}
  346: 
  347: 		var status string
  348: 		err := tx.QueryRow(
  349: 			ctx,
  350: 			`
  351: SELECT billing_status
  352: FROM tokenio_billing_charge_batches
  353: WHERE id = $1
  354: `,
  355: 			record.BillingChargeRequestID,
  356: 		).Scan(&status)
  357: 		if err != nil {
  358: 			if errors.Is(normalizeRegistryReadError(err), ports.ErrNotFound) {
  359: 				return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  360: 			}
  361: 			return normalizeRegistryReadError(err)
  362: 		}
  363: 		if domain.BillingChargeStatus(status) !=
  364: 			domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
  365: 			return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  366: 		}
  367: 	}
  368: 	return nil
  369: }
  370: 
  371: func insertChargeCommand(
  372: 	ctx context.Context,
  373: 	tx pgx.Tx,
  374: 	plan ports.UsageChargeBatchPlan,
  375: ) error {
  376: 	batch := plan.Batch
  377: 	if _, err := tx.Exec(
  378: 		ctx,
  379: 		insertBillingChargeBatchSQL,
  380: 		batch.ID,
  381: 		batch.UserID,
  382: 		batch.BillingSubjectUserID,
  383: 		string(batch.ProviderType),
  384: 		batch.ClientModel,
  385: 		batch.BillingModel,
  386: 		batch.InputTokens,
  387: 		batch.OutputTokens,
  388: 		batch.AmountCents,
  389: 		batch.Currency,
  390: 		string(batch.Status),
  391: 		nullableInt64(batch.BillingResponseBalanceCents),
  392: 		batch.BillingErrorCode,
  393: 		canonicalTime(batch.CreatedAt),
  394: 		canonicalTimePointer(batch.ChargedAt),
  395: 		canonicalTimePointer(batch.FailedAt),
  396: 		canonicalTime(batch.UpdatedAt),
  397: 	); err != nil {
  398: 		return NormalizeError(err)
  399: 	}
  400: 
  401: 	for index, allocation := range plan.Allocations {
  402: 		if _, err := tx.Exec(
  403: 			ctx,
  404: 			insertBillingChargeAllocationSQL,
  405: 			allocation.ID,
  406: 			allocation.BatchID,
  407: 			allocation.LocalRequestID,
  408: 			index,
  409: 			allocation.ChargedAmountCents,
  410: 			allocation.RemainingAmountCents,
  411: 			canonicalTime(allocation.CreatedAt),
  412: 		); err != nil {
  413: 			return NormalizeError(err)
  414: 		}
  415: 	}
  416: 	return nil
  417: }
  418: 
  419: func compareChargePlanReplay(
  420: 	plan ports.UsageChargeBatchPlan,
  421: 	persisted ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot,
  422: ) error {
  423: 	if !sameBatchCommand(plan.Batch, persisted.Batch) ||
  424: 		len(plan.Allocations) != len(persisted.Allocations) ||
  425: 		len(plan.ExpectedRecords) !=
  426: 			len(persisted.ExpectedRecords) {
  427: 		return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  428: 	}
  429: 
  430: 	for index := range plan.Allocations {
  431: 		if !sameAllocationCommand(
  432: 			plan.Allocations[index],
  433: 			persisted.Allocations[index],
  434: 		) {
  435: 			return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  436: 		}
  437: 		comparison := postClaimRecord(
  438: 			plan.ExpectedRecords[index],
  439: 			plan.Batch.ID,
  440: 		)
  441: 		if !sameUsageRecord(
  442: 			comparison,
  443: 			persisted.ExpectedRecords[index],
  444: 		) {
  445: 			return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  446: 		}
  447: 		if plan.Allocations[index].LocalRequestID !=
  448: 			plan.ExpectedRecords[index].LocalRequestID {
  449: 			return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  450: 		}
  451: 	}
  452: 	return nil
  453: }

FILE: internal/infrastructure/postgres/usage_charge_reconcile.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: f895c6d38d508d837c6e73dacc8a6d367e1b832a
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package postgres
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"time"
    6: 
    7: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
    8: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
    9: 	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
   10: )
   11: 
   12: func (r *UsageLedger) MarkChargeBatchFailed(
   13: 	ctx context.Context,
   14: 	batchID string,
   15: 	expectedStatus domain.BillingChargeStatus,
   16: 	billingErrorCode string,
   17: 	failedAt time.Time,
   18: ) error {
   19: 	if batchID == "" ||
   20: 		billingErrorCode == "" ||
   21: 		!isCanonicalUTCTime(failedAt) {
   22: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   23: 	}
   24: 	failedAt = canonicalTime(failedAt)
   25: 
   26: 	return InTx(
   27: 		ctx,
   28: 		r.db,
   29: 		pgx.TxOptions{IsoLevel: pgx.Serializable},
   30: 		func(tx pgx.Tx) error {
   31: 			batch, err := scanBillingChargeBatch(
   32: 				tx.QueryRow(
   33: 					ctx,
   34: 					findBillingChargeBatchForUpdateSQL,
   35: 					batchID,
   36: 				),
   37: 			)
   38: 			if err != nil {
   39: 				return err
   40: 			}
   41: 
   42: 			switch {
   43: 			case batch.Status ==
   44: 				domain.BillingChargeStatusPending &&
   45: 				expectedStatus ==
   46: 					domain.BillingChargeStatusPending:
   47: 				tag, err := tx.Exec(
   48: 					ctx,
   49: 					`
   50: UPDATE tokenio_billing_charge_batches
   51: SET billing_status = 'failed',
   52:     billing_error_code = $2,
   53:     failed_at = $3,
   54:     updated_at = $3
   55: WHERE id = $1
   56:   AND billing_status = 'pending'
   57: `,
   58: 					batchID,
   59: 					billingErrorCode,
   60: 					failedAt,
   61: 				)
   62: 				if err != nil {
   63: 					return NormalizeError(err)
   64: 				}
   65: 				if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
   66: 					return ports.ErrStoreConflict
   67: 				}
   68: 				return nil
   69: 
   70: 			case batch.Status ==
   71: 				domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed &&
   72: 				expectedStatus ==
   73: 					domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed &&
   74: 				batch.BillingErrorCode == billingErrorCode:
   75: 				return nil
   76: 
   77: 			default:
   78: 				return ports.ErrStoreConflict
   79: 			}
   80: 		},
   81: 	)
   82: }
   83: 
   84: func (r *UsageLedger) ApplyChargeSuccess(
   85: 	ctx context.Context,
   86: 	success ports.UsageChargeSuccess,
   87: ) error {
   88: 	if err := validateChargeSuccessPersistence(success); err != nil {
   89: 		return err
   90: 	}
   91: 
   92: 	return InTx(
   93: 		ctx,
   94: 		r.db,
   95: 		pgx.TxOptions{IsoLevel: pgx.Serializable},
   96: 		func(tx pgx.Tx) error {
   97: 			_, err := applyChargeSuccessInTx(
   98: 				ctx,
   99: 				tx,
  100: 				success,
  101: 				false,
  102: 			)
  103: 			return err
  104: 		},
  105: 	)
  106: }
  107: 
  108: func validateChargeSuccessPersistence(
  109: 	success ports.UsageChargeSuccess,
  110: ) error {
  111: 	if success.BatchID == "" ||
  112: 		!isCanonicalUTCTime(success.ChargedAt) ||
  113: 		len(success.Allocations) == 0 ||
  114: 		len(success.Allocations) !=
  115: 			len(success.ExpectedRecords) ||
  116: 		success.BillingBalanceCents != nil &&
  117: 			*success.BillingBalanceCents < 0 {
  118: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  119: 	}
  120: 	return nil
  121: }
  122: 
  123: func compareChargeSuccessMetadata(
  124: 	success ports.UsageChargeSuccess,
  125: 	persisted ports.BillingChargeBatchSnapshot,
  126: ) error {
  127: 	if success.BatchID != persisted.Batch.ID ||
  128: 		len(success.Allocations) != len(persisted.Allocations) ||
  129: 		len(success.ExpectedRecords) !=
  130: 			len(persisted.ExpectedRecords) {
  131: 		return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  132: 	}
  133: 
  134: 	for index := range success.Allocations {
  135: 		if !sameAllocationCommand(
  136: 			success.Allocations[index],
  137: 			persisted.Allocations[index],
  138: 		) ||
  139: 			!sameUsageRecord(
  140: 				success.ExpectedRecords[index],
  141: 				persisted.ExpectedRecords[index],
  142: 			) {
  143: 			return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  144: 		}
  145: 	}
  146: 	return nil
  147: }

FILE: internal/infrastructure/postgres/usage_charge_success_tx.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 5d92423a80ae7d1366d4b93dd356682eb2284dc2
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package postgres
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 
    6: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
    7: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
    8: 	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
    9: )
   10: 
   11: type chargeSuccessTxResult struct {
   12: 	Before domain.BillingChargeBatch
   13: 	After  domain.BillingChargeBatch
   14: 	Replay bool
   15: }
   16: 
   17: func applyChargeSuccessInTx(
   18: 	ctx context.Context,
   19: 	tx pgx.Tx,
   20: 	success ports.UsageChargeSuccess,
   21: 	requireFailed bool,
   22: ) (chargeSuccessTxResult, error) {
   23: 	persisted, err := loadBillingChargeSnapshot(
   24: 		ctx,
   25: 		tx,
   26: 		success.BatchID,
   27: 		true,
   28: 	)
   29: 	if err != nil {
   30: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, err
   31: 	}
   32: 	if err := compareChargeSuccessMetadata(
   33: 		success,
   34: 		persisted,
   35: 	); err != nil {
   36: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, err
   37: 	}
   38: 
   39: 	if persisted.Batch.Status ==
   40: 		domain.BillingChargeStatusSucceeded {
   41: 		if persisted.Batch.ChargedAt == nil ||
   42: 			!persisted.Batch.ChargedAt.Equal(
   43: 				success.ChargedAt,
   44: 			) ||
   45: 			!sameOptionalInt64(
   46: 				persisted.Batch.BillingResponseBalanceCents,
   47: 				success.BillingBalanceCents,
   48: 			) {
   49: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, ports.ErrStoreConflict
   50: 		}
   51: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{
   52: 			Before: persisted.Batch,
   53: 			After:  persisted.Batch,
   54: 			Replay: true,
   55: 		}, nil
   56: 	}
   57: 
   58: 	if requireFailed {
   59: 		if persisted.Batch.Status !=
   60: 			domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed {
   61: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
   62: 				ports.ErrStoreConflict
   63: 		}
   64: 	} else if persisted.Batch.Status !=
   65: 		domain.BillingChargeStatusPending &&
   66: 		persisted.Batch.Status !=
   67: 			domain.BillingChargeStatusFailed {
   68: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, ports.ErrStoreConflict
   69: 	}
   70: 
   71: 	currentByID, err := lockUsageRecordsForCharge(
   72: 		ctx,
   73: 		tx,
   74: 		persisted.ExpectedRecords,
   75: 	)
   76: 	if err != nil {
   77: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, err
   78: 	}
   79: 
   80: 	for index, expected := range persisted.ExpectedRecords {
   81: 		current, ok := currentByID[expected.LocalRequestID]
   82: 		if !ok || !sameUsageRecord(current, expected) {
   83: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
   84: 				ports.ErrStoreConflict
   85: 		}
   86: 		allocation := persisted.Allocations[index]
   87: 		if allocation.ChargedAmountCents >
   88: 			current.RemainingAmountCents ||
   89: 			allocation.RemainingAmountCents !=
   90: 				current.RemainingAmountCents-
   91: 					allocation.ChargedAmountCents {
   92: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
   93: 				ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   94: 		}
   95: 
   96: 		next := current
   97: 		next.ChargedAmountCents +=
   98: 			allocation.ChargedAmountCents
   99: 		next.RemainingAmountCents =
  100: 			allocation.RemainingAmountCents
  101: 		if next.RemainingAmountCents == 0 {
  102: 			next.Status = domain.UsageStatusCharged
  103: 		} else {
  104: 			next.Status =
  105: 				domain.UsageStatusPartiallyCharged
  106: 		}
  107: 		next.ChargedAt =
  108: 			cloneCanonicalTime(&success.ChargedAt)
  109: 		next.FailureReason = ""
  110: 		next.FailedAt = nil
  111: 		next.BillingChargeRequestID =
  112: 			persisted.Batch.ID
  113: 		next.UpdatedAt = canonicalTime(success.ChargedAt)
  114: 
  115: 		args := usageRecordNamedArgs(next)
  116: 		args["lookup_local_request_id"] =
  117: 			current.LocalRequestID
  118: 		args["expected_status"] = string(current.Status)
  119: 
  120: 		tag, err := tx.Exec(
  121: 			ctx,
  122: 			updateUsageRecordCASQL,
  123: 			args,
  124: 		)
  125: 		if err != nil {
  126: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
  127: 				NormalizeError(err)
  128: 		}
  129: 		if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
  130: 			return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
  131: 				ports.ErrStoreConflict
  132: 		}
  133: 	}
  134: 
  135: 	tag, err := tx.Exec(
  136: 		ctx,
  137: 		`
  138: UPDATE tokenio_billing_charge_batches
  139: SET billing_status = 'succeeded',
  140:     billing_response_balance_cents = $2,
  141:     billing_error_code = '',
  142:     charged_at = $3,
  143:     failed_at = NULL,
  144:     updated_at = $3
  145: WHERE id = $1
  146:   AND billing_status = $4
  147: `,
  148: 		success.BatchID,
  149: 		nullableInt64(success.BillingBalanceCents),
  150: 		canonicalTime(success.ChargedAt),
  151: 		string(persisted.Batch.Status),
  152: 	)
  153: 	if err != nil {
  154: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, NormalizeError(err)
  155: 	}
  156: 	if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
  157: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{},
  158: 			ports.ErrStoreConflict
  159: 	}
  160: 
  161: 	updated, err := scanBillingChargeBatch(
  162: 		tx.QueryRow(
  163: 			ctx,
  164: 			findBillingChargeBatchForUpdateSQL,
  165: 			success.BatchID,
  166: 		),
  167: 	)
  168: 	if err != nil {
  169: 		return chargeSuccessTxResult{}, err
  170: 	}
  171: 	return chargeSuccessTxResult{
  172: 		Before: persisted.Batch,
  173: 		After:  updated,
  174: 	}, nil
  175: }

FILE: internal/infrastructure/postgres/usage_reseller_billable_store.go
GIT_BLOB_SHA: 73745995baf537373a26e54616ff3eb49deaf379
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    1: package postgres
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"math"
    6: 	"strings"
    7: 	"time"
    8: 
    9: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
   10: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
   11: 	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
   12: )
   13: 
   14: const updateResellerBalanceReconcileSQL = `
   15: UPDATE tokenio_resellers
   16: SET
   17:     balance_cents = $2,
   18:     reserved_cents = $3,
   19:     updated_at = GREATEST(updated_at, $4)
   20: WHERE id = $1
   21: RETURNING
   22: ` + resellerBalanceColumns
   23: 
   24: var _ ports.UsageResellerBillableStore = (*UsageLedger)(nil)
   25: 
   26: func (r *UsageLedger) CommitReservedUsageAndReconcileReseller(
   27: 	ctx context.Context,
   28: 	localRequestID string,
   29: 	next domain.UsageRecord,
   30: ) (ports.UsageResellerBillableResult, error) {
   31: 	if err := validateUsageResellerBillableInput(
   32: 		localRequestID,
   33: 		next,
   34: 	); err != nil {
   35: 		return ports.UsageResellerBillableResult{}, err
   36: 	}
   37: 	canonicalNext := canonicalUsageBillableRecord(next)
   38: 
   39: 	var result ports.UsageResellerBillableResult
   40: 	err := InTx(
   41: 		ctx,
   42: 		r.db,
   43: 		pgx.TxOptions{},
   44: 		func(tx pgx.Tx) error {
   45: 			current, err := scanUsageRecord(tx.QueryRow(
   46: 				ctx,
   47: 				findUsageByLocalRequestIDForUpdateSQL,
   48: 				localRequestID,
   49: 			))
   50: 			if err != nil {
   51: 				return err
   52: 			}
   53: 
   54: 			if current.Status != domain.UsageStatusReserved {
   55: 				result = ports.UsageResellerBillableResult{
   56: 					Applied: false,
   57: 					Usage:   current,
   58: 				}
   59: 				return nil
   60: 			}
   61: 			if err := validatePersistedReservedUsageForReconciliation(
   62: 				current,
   63: 			); err != nil {
   64: 				return err
   65: 			}
   66: 			if !sameUsageReservationIdentity(current, canonicalNext) ||
   67: 				canonicalNext.UpdatedAt.Before(current.UpdatedAt) {
   68: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   69: 			}
   70: 
   71: 			reseller, err := scanReseller(tx.QueryRow(
   72: 				ctx,
   73: 				findResellerBalanceForUpdateSQL,
   74: 				current.SelectedResellerID,
   75: 			))
   76: 			if err != nil {
   77: 				return err
   78: 			}
   79: 			if reseller.ReservedCents <
   80: 				current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents {
   81: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   82: 			}
   83: 			if canonicalNext.ActualUpstreamCostCents > 0 &&
   84: 				reseller.BalanceCents < math.MinInt64+
   85: 					canonicalNext.ActualUpstreamCostCents {
   86: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   87: 			}
   88: 
   89: 			expectedReseller := reseller
   90: 			expectedReseller.ReservedCents -=
   91: 				current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents
   92: 			expectedReseller.BalanceCents -=
   93: 				canonicalNext.ActualUpstreamCostCents
   94: 			if canonicalNext.UpdatedAt.After(
   95: 				expectedReseller.UpdatedAt,
   96: 			) {
   97: 				expectedReseller.UpdatedAt = canonicalNext.UpdatedAt
   98: 			}
   99: 
  100: 			persistedReseller, err := scanReseller(tx.QueryRow(
  101: 				ctx,
  102: 				updateResellerBalanceReconcileSQL,
  103: 				current.SelectedResellerID,
  104: 				expectedReseller.BalanceCents,
  105: 				expectedReseller.ReservedCents,
  106: 				canonicalNext.UpdatedAt,
  107: 			))
  108: 			if err != nil {
  109: 				return err
  110: 			}
  111: 			if !sameResellerBalanceSnapshot(
  112: 				persistedReseller,
  113: 				expectedReseller,
  114: 			) {
  115: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  116: 			}
  117: 
  118: 			args := usageRecordNamedArgs(canonicalNext)
  119: 			args["lookup_local_request_id"] = localRequestID
  120: 			args["expected_status"] = string(
  121: 				domain.UsageStatusReserved,
  122: 			)
  123: 
  124: 			tag, err := tx.Exec(ctx, updateUsageRecordCASQL, args)
  125: 			if err != nil {
  126: 				return NormalizeError(err)
  127: 			}
  128: 			if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
  129: 				return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  130: 			}
  131: 
  132: 			result = ports.UsageResellerBillableResult{
  133: 				Applied:  true,
  134: 				Usage:    canonicalNext,
  135: 				Reseller: persistedReseller,
  136: 			}
  137: 			return nil
  138: 		},
  139: 	)
  140: 	if err != nil {
  141: 		return ports.UsageResellerBillableResult{}, err
  142: 	}
  143: 	return result, nil
  144: }
  145: 
  146: func validateUsageResellerBillableInput(
  147: 	localRequestID string,
  148: 	next domain.UsageRecord,
  149: ) error {
  150: 	if strings.TrimSpace(localRequestID) == "" ||
  151: 		next.LocalRequestID != localRequestID ||
  152: 		next.Status != domain.UsageStatusBillable ||
  153: 		next.Currency != "RUB" ||
  154: 		next.BillableAt == nil ||
  155: 		next.CreatedAt.IsZero() ||
  156: 		next.UpdatedAt.IsZero() ||
  157: 		next.ReservedAt == nil ||
  158: 		next.CreatedAt.Location() != time.UTC ||
  159: 		next.UpdatedAt.Location() != time.UTC ||
  160: 		next.ReservedAt.Location() != time.UTC ||
  161: 		next.BillableAt.Location() != time.UTC ||
  162: 		!next.BillableAt.Equal(next.UpdatedAt) ||
  163: 		next.UpdatedAt.Before(next.CreatedAt) ||
  164: 		next.ReleasedAt != nil ||
  165: 		next.ChargedAt != nil ||
  166: 		next.FailedAt != nil ||
  167: 		strings.TrimSpace(next.FailureReason) != "" ||
  168: 		strings.TrimSpace(next.BillingChargeRequestID) != "" ||
  169: 		next.ClientAmountCents < 0 ||
  170: 		next.ChargedAmountCents != 0 ||
  171: 		next.RemainingAmountCents != next.ClientAmountCents ||
  172: 		next.ActualUpstreamCostCents < 0 ||
  173: 		!validBillableUsageCompleteness(next.UsageCompleteness) ||
  174: 		!nonNegativeTokenUsage(next.Usage) {
  175: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  176: 	}
  177: 	return nil
  178: }
  179: 
  180: func validatePersistedReservedUsageForReconciliation(
  181: 	current domain.UsageRecord,
  182: ) error {
  183: 	if current.Status != domain.UsageStatusReserved ||
  184: 		strings.TrimSpace(current.SelectedResellerID) == "" ||
  185: 		current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents < 0 ||
  186: 		current.ReservedAt == nil ||
  187: 		current.BillableAt != nil ||
  188: 		current.ReleasedAt != nil ||
  189: 		current.ChargedAt != nil ||
  190: 		current.FailedAt != nil ||
  191: 		!nonNegativeTokenUsage(current.EstimatedUsage) ||
  192: 		current.ClientAmountCents != 0 ||
  193: 		current.ChargedAmountCents != 0 ||
  194: 		current.RemainingAmountCents != 0 ||
  195: 		current.ActualUpstreamCostCents != 0 ||
  196: 		current.Usage != (domain.TokenUsage{}) ||
  197: 		current.ProviderRequestID != "" ||
  198: 		current.ProviderResponseModel != "" ||
  199: 		current.FailureReason != "" ||
  200: 		current.BillingChargeRequestID != "" {
  201: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  202: 	}
  203: 	return nil
  204: }
  205: 
  206: func sameUsageReservationIdentity(
  207: 	current domain.UsageRecord,
  208: 	next domain.UsageRecord,
  209: ) bool {
  210: 	return current.LocalRequestID == next.LocalRequestID &&
  211: 		current.IdempotencyKey == next.IdempotencyKey &&
  212: 		current.UserID == next.UserID &&
  213: 		current.APIKeyID == next.APIKeyID &&
  214: 		current.APIFamily == next.APIFamily &&
  215: 		current.EndpointKind == next.EndpointKind &&
  216: 		current.ClientModel == next.ClientModel &&
  217: 		current.BillingModel == next.BillingModel &&
  218: 		current.SelectedResellerID == next.SelectedResellerID &&
  219: 		current.SelectedRouteID == next.SelectedRouteID &&
  220: 		current.ProviderType == next.ProviderType &&
  221: 		current.ProviderModel == next.ProviderModel &&
  222: 		current.EstimatedUsage == next.EstimatedUsage &&
  223: 		current.EstimatedClientAmountCents ==
  224: 			next.EstimatedClientAmountCents &&
  225: 		current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents ==
  226: 			next.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents &&
  227: 		current.Currency == next.Currency &&
  228: 		current.CreatedAt.Equal(next.CreatedAt) &&
  229: 		sameUsageBillableTimePointer(
  230: 			current.ReservedAt,
  231: 			next.ReservedAt,
  232: 		)
  233: }
  234: 
  235: func canonicalUsageBillableRecord(
  236: 	value domain.UsageRecord,
  237: ) domain.UsageRecord {
  238: 	result := value
  239: 	result.CreatedAt = postgresUsageTime(value.CreatedAt)
  240: 	result.UpdatedAt = postgresUsageTime(value.UpdatedAt)
  241: 	result.ReservedAt = postgresUsageTimePointer(value.ReservedAt)
  242: 	result.ReleasedAt = postgresUsageTimePointer(value.ReleasedAt)
  243: 	result.BillableAt = postgresUsageTimePointer(value.BillableAt)
  244: 	result.ChargedAt = postgresUsageTimePointer(value.ChargedAt)
  245: 	result.FailedAt = postgresUsageTimePointer(value.FailedAt)
  246: 	return result
  247: }
  248: 
  249: func postgresUsageTime(value time.Time) time.Time {
  250: 	if value.IsZero() {
  251: 		return value
  252: 	}
  253: 	return value.UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)
  254: }
  255: 
  256: func postgresUsageTimePointer(value *time.Time) *time.Time {
  257: 	if value == nil {
  258: 		return nil
  259: 	}
  260: 	canonical := postgresUsageTime(*value)
  261: 	return &canonical
  262: }
  263: 
  264: func sameUsageBillableTimePointer(
  265: 	left *time.Time,
  266: 	right *time.Time,
  267: ) bool {
  268: 	switch {
  269: 	case left == nil && right == nil:
  270: 		return true
  271: 	case left == nil || right == nil:
  272: 		return false
  273: 	default:
  274: 		return left.Equal(*right)
  275: 	}
  276: }
  277: 
  278: func validBillableUsageCompleteness(value string) bool {
  279: 	switch value {
  280: 	case "detailed", "aggregate", "estimated":
  281: 		return true
  282: 	default:
  283: 		return false
  284: 	}
  285: }
  286: 
  287: func nonNegativeTokenUsage(value domain.TokenUsage) bool {
  288: 	return value.InputTokens >= 0 &&
  289: 		value.CachedInputTokens >= 0 &&
  290: 		value.OutputTokens >= 0 &&
  291: 		value.ReasoningTokens >= 0 &&
  292: 		value.ImageInputTokens >= 0 &&
  293: 		value.AudioInputTokens >= 0 &&
  294: 		value.AudioOutputTokens >= 0 &&
  295: 		value.FileInputTokens >= 0 &&
  296: 		value.VideoInputTokens >= 0 &&
  297: 		value.ImageGenerationUnits >= 0
  298: }

   13: func TestUsageResellerBillableIntegration(t *testing.T) {
   14: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 40, 40)
   15: 	next := usageResellerBillableNext(t, fixture, 35)
   16: 
   17: 	committed, err := fixture.ledger.
   18: 		CommitReservedUsageAndReconcileReseller(
   19: 			t.Context(),
   20: 			fixture.localRequestID,
   21: 			next,
   22: 		)
   23: 	if err != nil {
   24: 		t.Fatalf("commit usage and reconcile reseller: %v", err)
   25: 	}
   26: 	if !committed.Applied ||
   27: 		committed.Usage.Status != domain.UsageStatusBillable ||
   28: 		committed.Usage.ActualUpstreamCostCents != 35 ||
   29: 		committed.Reseller.BalanceCents != 965 ||
   30: 		committed.Reseller.ReservedCents != 0 {
   31: 		t.Fatalf("committed result = %+v", committed)
   32: 	}
   33: 
   34: 	replayed, err := fixture.ledger.
   35: 		CommitReservedUsageAndReconcileReseller(
   36: 			t.Context(),
   37: 			fixture.localRequestID,
   38: 			next,
   39: 		)
   40: 	if err != nil {
   41: 		t.Fatalf("commit replay: %v", err)
   42: 	}
   43: 	if replayed.Applied ||
   44: 		replayed.Usage.Status != domain.UsageStatusBillable {
   45: 		t.Fatalf("replayed result = %+v", replayed)
   46: 	}
   47: 
   48: 	currentReseller := fixture.loadReseller(t)
   49: 	if currentReseller.BalanceCents != 965 ||
   50: 		currentReseller.ReservedCents != 0 {
   51: 		t.Fatalf("current reseller = %+v", currentReseller)
   52: 	}
   53: }

   76: 
   77: func TestUsageResellerBillableConcurrentReplayIntegration(
   78: 	t *testing.T,
   79: ) {
   80: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 40, 40)
   81: 	next := usageResellerBillableNext(t, fixture, 35)
   82: 
   83: 	type callResult struct {
   84: 		value ports.UsageResellerBillableResult
   85: 		err   error
   86: 	}
   87: 
   88: 	start := make(chan struct{})
   89: 	results := make(chan callResult, 2)
   90: 	var ready sync.WaitGroup
   91: 	ready.Add(2)
   92: 
   93: 	for range 2 {
   94: 		go func() {
   95: 			ready.Done()
   96: 			<-start
   97: 			value, callErr := fixture.ledger.
   98: 				CommitReservedUsageAndReconcileReseller(
   99: 					t.Context(),
  100: 					fixture.localRequestID,
  101: 					next,
  102: 				)
  103: 			results <- callResult{
  104: 				value: value,
  105: 				err:   callErr,
  106: 			}
  107: 		}()
  108: 	}
  109: 
  110: 	ready.Wait()
  111: 	close(start)
  112: 
  113: 	applied := 0
  114: 	for range 2 {
  115: 		call := <-results
  116: 		if call.err != nil {
  117: 			t.Fatalf("concurrent commit error: %v", call.err)
  118: 		}
  119: 		if call.value.Applied {
  120: 			applied++
  121: 		}
  122: 	}
  123: 	if applied != 1 {
  124: 		t.Fatalf("applied commit count = %d, want 1", applied)
  125: 	}
  126: 
  127: 	currentReseller := fixture.loadReseller(t)
  128: 	if currentReseller.BalanceCents != 965 ||
  129: 		currentReseller.ReservedCents != 0 {
  130: 		t.Fatalf("current reseller = %+v", currentReseller)
  131: 	}
  132: }
  133: 
  134: func TestUsageResellerBillableUnderflowRollsBackIntegration(
  135: 	t *testing.T,
  136: ) {
  137: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 39, 40)
  138: 	next := usageResellerBillableNext(t, fixture, 35)
  139: 
  140: 	_, err := fixture.ledger.
  141: 		CommitReservedUsageAndReconcileReseller(
  142: 			t.Context(),
  143: 			fixture.localRequestID,
  144: 			next,
  145: 		)
  146: 	if !errors.Is(err, ports.ErrStoreContractViolation) {
  147: 		t.Fatalf(
  148: 			"underflow error = %v, want contract violation",
  149: 			err,
  150: 		)
  151: 	}
  152: 
  153: 	currentReseller := fixture.loadReseller(t)
  154: 	if currentReseller.BalanceCents != 1000 ||
  155: 		currentReseller.ReservedCents != 39 {
  156: 		t.Fatalf("current reseller = %+v", currentReseller)
  157: 	}
  158: 	currentUsage, findErr := fixture.ledger.FindByLocalRequestID(
  159: 		t.Context(),
  160: 		fixture.localRequestID,
  161: 	)
  162: 	if findErr != nil {
  163: 		t.Fatalf("find usage after rollback: %v", findErr)
  164: 	}
  165: 	if currentUsage.Status != domain.UsageStatusReserved ||
  166: 		currentUsage.BillableAt != nil {
  167: 		t.Fatalf("current usage = %+v", currentUsage)
  168: 	}
  169: }

  197: 
  198: func usageResellerBillableNext(
  199: 	t *testing.T,
  200: 	fixture usageResellerReleaseFixture,
  201: 	actualUpstreamCostCents int64,
  202: ) domain.UsageRecord {
  203: 	t.Helper()
  204: 
  205: 	current, err := fixture.ledger.FindByLocalRequestID(
  206: 		t.Context(),
  207: 		fixture.localRequestID,
  208: 	)
  209: 	if err != nil {
  210: 		t.Fatalf("FindByLocalRequestID: %v", err)
  211: 	}
  212: 
  213: 	billableAt := fixture.now.Add(time.Second)
  214: 	next := *current
  215: 	next.Status = domain.UsageStatusBillable
  216: 	next.Usage = domain.TokenUsage{
  217: 		InputTokens:  8,
  218: 		OutputTokens: 4,
  219: 	}
  220: 	next.UsageCompleteness = "detailed"
  221: 	next.ClientAmountCents = 90
  222: 	next.ChargedAmountCents = 0
  223: 	next.RemainingAmountCents = 90
  224: 	next.ActualUpstreamCostCents = actualUpstreamCostCents
  225: 	next.ProviderRequestID = "provider-request"
  226: 	next.ProviderResponseModel = next.ProviderModel
  227: 	next.BillableAt = &billableAt
  228: 	next.UpdatedAt = billableAt
  229: 	return next
  230: }

    1: package postgres
    2: 
    3: import (
    4: 	"context"
    5: 	"strings"
    6: 	"time"
    7: 
    8: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/domain"
    9: 	"github.com/bogachenko/tokenio-gateway/internal/ports"
   10: 	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
   11: )
   12: 
   13: const updateResellerBalanceReleaseSQL = `
   14: UPDATE tokenio_resellers
   15: SET
   16:     reserved_cents = $2,
   17:     updated_at = GREATEST(updated_at, $3)
   18: WHERE id = $1
   19: RETURNING
   20: ` + resellerBalanceColumns
   21: 
   22: var _ ports.UsageResellerReleaseStore = (*UsageLedger)(nil)
   23: 
   24: func (r *UsageLedger) ReleaseReservedUsageAndResellerReserve(
   25: 	ctx context.Context,
   26: 	localRequestID string,
   27: 	failureReason string,
   28: 	releasedAt time.Time,
   29: ) (ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult, error) {
   30: 	if err := validateUsageResellerReleaseInput(
   31: 		localRequestID,
   32: 		failureReason,
   33: 		releasedAt,
   34: 	); err != nil {
   35: 		return ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult{}, err
   36: 	}
   37: 
   38: 	canonicalReleasedAt := releasedAt.UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)
   39: 	var result ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult
   40: 
   41: 	err := InTx(
   42: 		ctx,
   43: 		r.db,
   44: 		pgx.TxOptions{},
   45: 		func(tx pgx.Tx) error {
   46: 			current, err := scanUsageRecord(tx.QueryRow(
   47: 				ctx,
   48: 				findUsageByLocalRequestIDForUpdateSQL,
   49: 				localRequestID,
   50: 			))
   51: 			if err != nil {
   52: 				return err
   53: 			}
   54: 
   55: 			if current.Status != domain.UsageStatusReserved {
   56: 				result = ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult{
   57: 					Applied: false,
   58: 					Usage:   current,
   59: 				}
   60: 				return nil
   61: 			}
   62: 			if strings.TrimSpace(current.SelectedResellerID) == "" ||
   63: 				current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents < 0 ||
   64: 				canonicalReleasedAt.Before(current.UpdatedAt) {
   65: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   66: 			}
   67: 
   68: 			reseller, err := scanReseller(tx.QueryRow(
   69: 				ctx,
   70: 				findResellerBalanceForUpdateSQL,
   71: 				current.SelectedResellerID,
   72: 			))
   73: 			if err != nil {
   74: 				return err
   75: 			}
   76: 			if reseller.ReservedCents <
   77: 				current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents {
   78: 				return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
   79: 			}
   80: 
   81: 			if current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents > 0 {
   82: 				expectedReseller := reseller
   83: 				expectedReseller.ReservedCents -=
   84: 					current.EstimatedUpstreamCostCents
   85: 				if canonicalReleasedAt.After(
   86: 					expectedReseller.UpdatedAt,
   87: 				) {
   88: 					expectedReseller.UpdatedAt = canonicalReleasedAt
   89: 				}
   90: 
   91: 				persistedReseller, err := scanReseller(tx.QueryRow(
   92: 					ctx,
   93: 					updateResellerBalanceReleaseSQL,
   94: 					current.SelectedResellerID,
   95: 					expectedReseller.ReservedCents,
   96: 					canonicalReleasedAt,
   97: 				))
   98: 				if err != nil {
   99: 					return err
  100: 				}
  101: 				if !sameResellerBalanceSnapshot(
  102: 					persistedReseller,
  103: 					expectedReseller,
  104: 				) {
  105: 					return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  106: 				}
  107: 			}
  108: 
  109: 			desired := current
  110: 			desired.Status = domain.UsageStatusReleased
  111: 			desired.FailureReason = failureReason
  112: 			desired.ReleasedAt = &canonicalReleasedAt
  113: 			desired.UpdatedAt = canonicalReleasedAt
  114: 
  115: 			args := usageRecordNamedArgs(desired)
  116: 			args["lookup_local_request_id"] = localRequestID
  117: 			args["expected_status"] = string(
  118: 				domain.UsageStatusReserved,
  119: 			)
  120: 
  121: 			tag, err := tx.Exec(ctx, updateUsageRecordCASQL, args)
  122: 			if err != nil {
  123: 				return NormalizeError(err)
  124: 			}
  125: 			if tag.RowsAffected() != 1 {
  126: 				return ports.ErrStoreConflict
  127: 			}
  128: 
  129: 			result = ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult{
  130: 				Applied: true,
  131: 				Usage:   desired,
  132: 			}
  133: 			return nil
  134: 		},
  135: 	)
  136: 	if err != nil {
  137: 		return ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult{}, err
  138: 	}
  139: 	return result, nil
  140: }
  141: 
  142: func validateUsageResellerReleaseInput(
  143: 	localRequestID string,
  144: 	failureReason string,
  145: 	releasedAt time.Time,
  146: ) error {
  147: 	if strings.TrimSpace(localRequestID) == "" ||
  148: 		strings.TrimSpace(failureReason) == "" ||
  149: 		releasedAt.IsZero() ||
  150: 		releasedAt.Location() != time.UTC {
  151: 		return ports.ErrStoreContractViolation
  152: 	}
  153: 	return nil
  154: }

   16: func TestUsageResellerReleaseIntegration(t *testing.T) {
   17: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 40, 40)
   18: 	releasedAt := fixture.now.Add(time.Second)
   19: 
   20: 	released, err := fixture.ledger.
   21: 		ReleaseReservedUsageAndResellerReserve(
   22: 			t.Context(),
   23: 			fixture.localRequestID,
   24: 			"connection_error",
   25: 			releasedAt,
   26: 		)
   27: 	if err != nil {
   28: 		t.Fatalf("release usage and reseller reserve: %v", err)
   29: 	}
   30: 	if !released.Applied ||
   31: 		released.Usage.Status != domain.UsageStatusReleased ||
   32: 		released.Usage.FailureReason != "connection_error" ||
   33: 		released.Usage.ReleasedAt == nil ||
   34: 		!released.Usage.ReleasedAt.Equal(releasedAt) {
   35: 		t.Fatalf("released result = %+v", released)
   36: 	}
   37: 
   38: 	replayed, err := fixture.ledger.
   39: 		ReleaseReservedUsageAndResellerReserve(
   40: 			t.Context(),
   41: 			fixture.localRequestID,
   42: 			"connection_error",
   43: 			releasedAt,
   44: 		)
   45: 	if err != nil {
   46: 		t.Fatalf("release replay: %v", err)
   47: 	}
   48: 	if replayed.Applied ||
   49: 		replayed.Usage.Status != domain.UsageStatusReleased ||
   50: 		replayed.Usage.FailureReason != "connection_error" {
   51: 		t.Fatalf("replayed result = %+v", replayed)
   52: 	}
   53: 
   54: 	currentReseller := fixture.loadReseller(t)
   55: 	if currentReseller.BalanceCents != 1000 ||
   56: 		currentReseller.ReservedCents != 0 {
   57: 		t.Fatalf("current reseller = %+v", currentReseller)
   58: 	}
   59: }
   60: 
   61: func TestUsageResellerReleaseConcurrentReplayIntegration(
   62: 	t *testing.T,
   63: ) {
   64: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 40, 40)
   65: 	releasedAt := fixture.now.Add(time.Second)
   66: 
   67: 	type callResult struct {
   68: 		value ports.UsageResellerReleaseResult
   69: 		err   error
   70: 	}
   71: 
   72: 	start := make(chan struct{})
   73: 	results := make(chan callResult, 2)
   74: 	var ready sync.WaitGroup
   75: 	ready.Add(2)
   76: 
   77: 	for range 2 {
   78: 		go func() {
   79: 			ready.Done()
   80: 			<-start
   81: 			value, callErr := fixture.ledger.
   82: 				ReleaseReservedUsageAndResellerReserve(
   83: 					t.Context(),
   84: 					fixture.localRequestID,
   85: 					"connection_error",
   86: 					releasedAt,
   87: 				)
   88: 			results <- callResult{
   89: 				value: value,
   90: 				err:   callErr,
   91: 			}
   92: 		}()
   93: 	}
   94: 
   95: 	ready.Wait()
   96: 	close(start)
   97: 
   98: 	applied := 0
   99: 	for range 2 {
  100: 		call := <-results
  101: 		if call.err != nil {
  102: 			t.Fatalf("concurrent release error: %v", call.err)
  103: 		}
  104: 		if call.value.Applied {
  105: 			applied++
  106: 		}
  107: 	}
  108: 	if applied != 1 {
  109: 		t.Fatalf("applied release count = %d, want 1", applied)
  110: 	}
  111: 
  112: 	currentReseller := fixture.loadReseller(t)
  113: 	if currentReseller.ReservedCents != 0 {
  114: 		t.Fatalf("current reseller = %+v", currentReseller)
  115: 	}
  116: 	currentUsage, err := fixture.ledger.FindByLocalRequestID(
  117: 		t.Context(),
  118: 		fixture.localRequestID,
  119: 	)
  120: 	if err != nil {
  121: 		t.Fatalf("find released usage: %v", err)
  122: 	}
  123: 	if currentUsage.Status != domain.UsageStatusReleased {
  124: 		t.Fatalf("current usage = %+v", currentUsage)
  125: 	}
  126: }
  127: 
  128: func TestUsageResellerReleaseUnderflowRollsBackIntegration(
  129: 	t *testing.T,
  130: ) {
  131: 	fixture := newUsageResellerReleaseFixture(t, 39, 40)

  136: 			fixture.localRequestID,

  178:

  181: 		t.Skip("TOKENIO_TEST_DATABASE_DSN is not set")
  182: 	}

  184: 	ctx := t.Context()

  187: 		t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
  188: 	}

  191: 		t.Fatalf("ApplyMigrations: %v", err)
  192: 	}

  195: 	if err != nil {
  196: 		t.Fatalf("NewUsageLedger: %v", err)
  197: 	}

  199: 	if err != nil {
  200: 		t.Fatalf("NewResellerRepository: %v", err)
  201: 	}

  204: 	userID := "usage-release-user-" + suffix
  205: 	keyID := "usage-release-key-" + suffix
  206: 	resellerID := "usage-release-reseller-" + suffix
  207: 	routeID := "usage-release-route-" + suffix
  208: 	localRequestID := "usage-release-request-" + suffix
  209: 	now := time.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)
  210: 
  211: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
  212: 		cleanupCtx := context.Background()
  213: 		for _, statement := range []struct {
  214: 			sql string
  215: 			arg string
  216: 		}{
  217: 			{
  218: 				"DELETE FROM tokenio_usage_records WHERE user_id = $1",
  219: 				userID,
  220: 			},
  221: 			{
  222: 				"DELETE FROM tokenio_routes WHERE id = $1",
  223: 				routeID,
  224: 			},
  225: 			{
  226: 				"DELETE FROM tokenio_resellers WHERE id = $1",
  227: 				resellerID,
  228: 			},
  229: 			{
  230: 				"DELETE FROM tokenio_api_keys WHERE id = $1",
  231: 				keyID,
  232: 			},
  233: 			{
  234: 				"DELETE FROM tokenio_users WHERE id = $1",
  235: 				userID,
  236: 			},
  237: 		} {
  238: 			_, _ = db.Exec(cleanupCtx, statement.sql, statement.arg)
  239: 		}
  240: 	})
  241: 
  242: 	if _, err := db.Exec(
  243: 		ctx,
  244: 		`
  245: INSERT INTO tokenio_users (
  246:     id,
  247:     external_billing_user_id,
  248:     created_at,
  249:     updated_at
  250: )
  251: VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $3)
  252: `,
  253: 		userID,
  254: 		"billing-"+suffix,
  255: 		now,
  256: 	); err != nil {
  257: 		t.Fatalf("insert user: %v", err)
  258: 	}
  259: 	if _, err := db.Exec(
  260: 		ctx,
  261: 		`
  262: INSERT INTO tokenio_api_keys (
  263:     id,
  264:     user_id,
  265:     name,
  266:     key_hash,
  267:     key_prefix,
  268:     created_at,
  269:     updated_at
  270: )
  271: VALUES ($1, $2, 'release-test', $3, 'sk_test', $4, $4)
  272: `,
  273: 		keyID,
  274: 		userID,
  275: 		"usage-release-hash-"+suffix,
  276: 		now,
  277: 	); err != nil {
  278: 		t.Fatalf("insert api key: %v", err)
  279: 	}
  280: 	if _, err := db.Exec(
  281: 		ctx,
  282: 		`
  283: INSERT INTO tokenio_resellers (
  284:     id,
  285:     name,
  286:     provider_type,
  287:     base_url,
  288:     api_key_env,
  289:     balance_cents,
  290:     reserved_cents,
  291:     created_at,
  292:     updated_at
  293: )
  294: VALUES (
  295:     $1,
  296:     'release-test',
  297:     'openai',
  298:     'https://example.test',
  299:     $2,
  300:     1000,
  301:     $3,
  302:     $4,
  303:     $4
  304: )
  305: `,
  306: 		resellerID,
  307: 		"USAGE_RELEASE_TEST_KEY_"+suffix,
  308: 		reservedCents,
  309: 		now,
  310: 	); err != nil {
  311: 		t.Fatalf("insert reseller: %v", err)
  312: 	}
  313: 	if _, err := db.Exec(
  314: 		ctx,
  315: 		`
  316: INSERT INTO tokenio_routes (
  317:     id,
  318:     reseller_id,
  319:     provider_type,
  320:     api_family,
  321:     endpoint_kind,
  322:     client_model,
  323:     provider_model,
  324:     created_at,
  325:     updated_at
  326: )
  327: VALUES (
  328:     $1,
  329:     $2,
  330:     'openai',
  331:     'openai_compatible',
  332:     'chat',
  333:     $3,
  334:     $3,
  335:     $4,
  336:     $4
  337: )
  338: `,
  339: 		routeID,
  340: 		resellerID,
  341: 		"usage-release-model-"+suffix,
  342: 		now,
  343: 	); err != nil {
  344: 		t.Fatalf("insert route: %v", err)
  345: 	}
  346: 
  347: 	reservedAt := now
  348: 	record := domain.UsageRecord{
  349: 		LocalRequestID:     localRequestID,
  350: 		IdempotencyKey:     "usage-release-idempotency-" + suffix,
  351: 		UserID:             userID,
  352: 		APIKeyID:           keyID,
  353: 		APIFamily:          domain.APIFamilyOpenAICompatible,
  354: 		EndpointKind:       domain.EndpointChat,
  355: 		ClientModel:        "usage-release-model-" + suffix,
  356: 		BillingModel:       "openai:usage-release-model-" + suffix,
  357: 		SelectedRouteID:    routeID,
  358: 		SelectedResellerID: resellerID,
  359: 		ProviderType:       domain.ProviderOpenAI,
  360: 		ProviderModel:      "usage-release-model-" + suffix,
  361: 		EstimatedUsage: domain.TokenUsage{
  362: 			InputTokens:  10,
  363: 			OutputTokens: 5,
  364: 		},
  365: 		EstimatedClientAmountCents: 100,
  366: 		EstimatedUpstreamCostCents: estimatedUpstreamCostCents,
  367: 		Currency:                   "RUB",
  368: 		UsageCompleteness:          "missing",
  369: 		Status:                     domain.UsageStatusReserved,
  370: 		CreatedAt:                  now,
  371: 		ReservedAt:                 &reservedAt,
  372: 		UpdatedAt:                  now,
  373: 	}
  374: 	created, err := ledger.CreateReserved(ctx, record)
  375: 	if err != nil {
  376: 		t.Fatalf("CreateReserved: %v", err)
  377: 	}
  378: 	if created.Outcome != ports.UsageReserveOutcomeCreated {
  379: 		t.Fatalf("created usage result = %+v", created)
  380: 	}
  381: 
  382: 	return usageResellerReleaseFixture{
  383: 		ledger:         ledger,
  384: 		resellers:      resellers,
  385: 		localRequestID: localRequestID,
  386: 		resellerID:     resellerID,
  387: 		now:            now,
  388: 	}
  389: }
  390: 
  391: func (f usageResellerReleaseFixture) loadReseller(
  392: 	t *testing.T,
  393: ) domain.Reseller {
  394: 	t.Helper()
  395: 
  396: 	values, err := f.resellers.FindByIDs(
  397: 		t.Context(),
  398: 		[]string{f.resellerID},
  399: 	)
  400: 	if err != nil {
  401: 		t.Fatalf("FindByIDs: %v", err)
  402: 	}
  403: 	value, exists := values[f.resellerID]
  404: 	if !exists {
  405: 		t.Fatalf("reseller %q not found", f.resellerID)
  406: 	}
  407: 	return value
  408: }

   20: type fixedGenerator struct {
   21: 	raw   string
   22: 	err   error
   23: 	calls int
   24: }
   25: 
   26: func (g *fixedGenerator) GenerateAPIKey() (string, error) {
   27: 	g.calls++
   28: 	return g.raw, g.err
   29: }
   30: 
   31: type hmacHasher struct {
   32: 	secret []byte
   33: }
   34: 
   35: func (h hmacHasher) Hash(raw string) string {
   36: 	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, h.secret)
   37: 	_, _ = mac.Write([]byte(raw))
   38: 	return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
   39: }
   40: 
   41: func testRawAPIKey(fill byte) string {
   42: 	return rawAPIKeyPrefix +
   43: 		base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(
   44: 			bytes.Repeat([]byte{fill}, 32),
   45: 		)
   46: }
   47:

   75: func newTestService(
   76: 	t *testing.T,
   77: 	generator ports.APIKeyGenerator,
   78: 	nonceSource []byte,
   79: ) *Service {
   80: 	t.Helper()
   81: 	service, err := newWithNonceReader(
   82: 		bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x42}, 32),
   83: 		"v1",
   84: 		generator,
   85: 		hmacHasher{secret: []byte("hash-secret")},
   86: 		bytes.NewReader(nonceSource),
   87: 	)

   89: 		t.Fatalf("newWithNonceReader: %v", err)
   90: 	}
   91: 	return service
   92: }

   94: func materialEnvelope(
   95: 	material ports.APIKeyProvisioningMaterial,
   96: 	request ports.APIKeyProvisioningMaterialRequest,
   97: ) (domain.APIKeyProvisioning, domain.APIKeyRecord) {
   98: 	expiresAt := request.ExpiresAt
   99: 	return domain.APIKeyProvisioning{
  100: 		ID:                    request.ProvisioningID,
  101: 		IdempotencyKey:        "payment-1",
  102: 		SourceReferenceHash:   strings.Repeat("a", 64),
  103: 		ExternalBillingUserID: request.User.ExternalBillingUserID,
  104: 		UserID:                request.User.ID,
  105: 		APIKeyID:              request.APIKeyID,
  106: 		ResultType:            domain.APIKeyProvisioningResultTypeKeyCreated,
  107: 		Status:                domain.APIKeyProvisioningStatusPendingDelivery,
  108: 		EncryptedRawKey:       material.EncryptedRawKey,
  109: 		EncryptionNonce:       material.EncryptionNonce,
  110: 		EncryptionKeyVersion:  material.EncryptionKeyVersion,
  111: 		CreatedAt:             request.CreatedAt,
  112: 		UpdatedAt:             request.CreatedAt,
  113: 		ExpiresAt:             &expiresAt,
  114: 	}, material.APIKey
  115: }
  116: 
  117: func TestCreateAndDecryptProvisioningMaterial(t *testing.T) {
  118: 	rawAPIKey := testRawAPIKey(0x7f)
  119: 	generator := &fixedGenerator{raw: rawAPIKey}
  120: 	service := newTestService(
  121: 		t,
  122: 		generator,
  123: 		bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x11}, 12),
  124: 	)
  125: 	request := testMaterialRequest()
  126: 
  127: 	material, err := service.CreateProvisioningMaterial(
  128: 		context.Background(),
  129: 		request,
  130: 	)
  131: 	if err != nil {
  132: 		t.Fatalf("CreateProvisioningMaterial: %v", err)
  133: 	}
  134: 	if generator.calls != 1 {
  135: 		t.Fatalf("generator calls = %d, want 1", generator.calls)
  136: 	}
  137: 	if material.APIKey.ID != request.APIKeyID ||
  138: 		material.APIKey.UserID != request.User.ID ||
  139: 		material.APIKey.Name != request.KeyName ||
  140: 		!material.APIKey.Enabled ||
  141: 		material.APIKey.KeyHash == "" ||
  142: 		material.APIKey.KeyPrefix !=
  143: 			displayPrefix(rawAPIKey) {
  144: 		t.Fatalf("API key record = %+v", material.APIKey)
  145: 	}
  146: 	if bytes.Contains(
  147: 		material.EncryptedRawKey,
  148: 		[]byte(rawAPIKey),
  149: 	) {
  150: 		t.Fatal("ciphertext contains plaintext API key")
  151: 	}
  152: 	if len(material.EncryptionNonce) != service.aead.NonceSize() {
  153: 		t.Fatalf(
  154: 			"nonce length = %d, want %d",
  155: 			len(material.EncryptionNonce),
  156: 			service.aead.NonceSize(),
  157: 		)
  158: 	}
  159: 	if material.EncryptionKeyVersion != "v1" {
  160: 		t.Fatalf(
  161: 			"key version = %q",
  162: 			material.EncryptionKeyVersion,
  163: 		)
  164: 	}
  165: 
  166: 	provisioning, apiKey := materialEnvelope(material, request)
  167: 	decrypted, err := service.DecryptProvisioningMaterial(
  168: 		context.Background(),
  169: 		provisioning,
  170: 		apiKey,
  171: 	)
  172: 	if err != nil {
  173: 		t.Fatalf("DecryptProvisioningMaterial: %v", err)
  174: 	}
  175: 	if decrypted != rawAPIKey {
  176: 		t.Fatalf("decrypted API key does not match generated key")
  177: 	}
  178: }
  179: 
  180: func TestCreateProvisioningMaterialUsesUniqueNonce(t *testing.T) {
  181: 	rawAPIKey := testRawAPIKey(0x31)
  182: 	firstNonce := bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x01}, 12)
  183: 	secondNonce := bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x02}, 12)
  184: 	service := newTestService(
  185: 		t,
  186: 		&fixedGenerator{raw: rawAPIKey},
  187: 		append(firstNonce, secondNonce...),
  188: 	)
  189: 	request := testMaterialRequest()
  190: 
  191: 	first, err := service.CreateProvisioningMaterial(
  192: 		context.Background(),
  193: 		request,
  194: 	)
  195: 	if err != nil {
  196: 		t.Fatal(err)
  197: 	}
  198: 	second, err := service.CreateProvisioningMaterial(
  199: 		context.Background(),
  200: 		request,
  201: 	)
  202: 	if err != nil {
  203: 		t.Fatal(err)
  204: 	}
  205: 	if bytes.Equal(
  206: 		first.EncryptionNonce,
  207: 		second.EncryptionNonce,
  208: 	) {
  209: 		t.Fatal("provisioning nonces are equal")
  210: 	}
  211: 	if bytes.Equal(
  212: 		first.EncryptedRawKey,
  213: 		second.EncryptedRawKey,
  214: 	) {
  215: 		t.Fatal("ciphertexts are equal for different nonces")
  216: 	}
  217: }

  219: func TestAuthenticatedDataRejectsRecordSubstitution(t *testing.T) {
  220: 	rawAPIKey := testRawAPIKey(0x41)
  221: 	service := newTestService(
  222: 		t,
  223: 		&fixedGenerator{raw: rawAPIKey},
  224: 		bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x03}, 12),
  225: 	)
  226: 	request := testMaterialRequest()
  227: 	material, err := service.CreateProvisioningMaterial(
  228: 		context.Background(),
  229: 		request,
  230: 	)
  231: 	if err != nil {
  232: 		t.Fatal(err)
  233: 	}
  234: 	originalProvisioning, originalAPIKey :=
  235: 		materialEnvelope(material, request)
  236:

  238: 		name         string

  240: 		apiKey       domain.APIKeyRecord
  241: 	}{
  242: 		{
  243: 			name: "provisioning ID",

  245: 				value := originalProvisioning
  246: 				value.ID = "prov_other"
  247: 				return value
  248: 			}(),
  249: 			apiKey: originalAPIKey,
  250: 		},
  251: 		{
  252: 			name: "API key ID",
  253: 			provisioning: func() domain.APIKeyProvisioning {
  254: 				value := originalProvisioning
  255: 				value.APIKeyID = "ak_other"
  256: 				return value
  257: 			}(),
  258: 			apiKey: func() domain.APIKeyRecord {
  259: 				value := originalAPIKey
  260: 				value.ID = "ak_other"
  261: 				return value
  262: 			}(),
  263: 		},
  264: 		{
  265: 			name: "user ID",
  266: 			provisioning: func() domain.APIKeyProvisioning {
  267: 				value := originalProvisioning
  268: 				value.UserID = "usr_other"
  269: 				return value
  270: 			}(),
  271: 			apiKey: func() domain.APIKeyRecord {
  272: 				value := originalAPIKey
  273: 				value.UserID = "usr_other"
  274: 				return value
  275: 			}(),
  276: 		},
  277: 	}
  278: 
  279: 	for _, test := range tests {
  280: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
  281: 			_, err := service.DecryptProvisioningMaterial(
  282: 				context.Background(),
  283: 				test.provisioning,
  284: 				test.apiKey,
  285: 			)
  286: 			if !errors.Is(err, ErrDecryptionFailed) {
  287: 				t.Fatalf(
  288: 					"error = %v, want ErrDecryptionFailed",
  289: 					err,
  290: 				)
  291: 			}
  292: 		})
  293: 	}
  294: }

  296: func TestDecryptRejectsPersistedContractMismatch(t *testing.T) {
  297: 	rawAPIKey := testRawAPIKey(0x55)
  298: 	service := newTestService(
  299: 		t,
  300: 		&fixedGenerator{raw: rawAPIKey},
  301: 		bytes.Repeat([]byte{0x04}, 12),
  302: 	)
  303: 	request := testMaterialRequest()
  304: 	material, err := service.CreateProvisioningMaterial(
  305: 		context.Background(),
  306: 		request,
  307: 	)
  308: 	if err != nil {
  309: 		t.Fatal(err)
  310: 	}
  311: 	originalProvisioning, originalAPIKey :=
  312: 		materialEnvelope(material, request)
  313: 
  314: 	tests := []struct {
  315: 		name         string
  316: 		provisioning domain.APIKeyProvisioning
  317: 		apiKey       domain.APIKeyRecord
  318: 	}{
  319: 		{
  320: 			name: "terminal status",
  321: 			provisioning: func() domain.APIKeyProvisioning {
  322: 				value := originalProvisioning
  323: 				value.Status =
  324: 					domain.APIKeyProvisioningStatusDelivered
  325: 				return value
  326: 			}(),
  327: 			apiKey: originalAPIKey,
  328: 		},
  329: 		{
  330: 			name: "wrong key version",
  331: 			provisioning: func() domain.APIKeyProvisioning {
  332: 				value := originalProvisioning
  333: 				value.EncryptionKeyVersion = "v2"
  334: 				return value
  335: 			}(),
  336: 			apiKey: originalAPIKey,
  337: 		},
  338: 		{
  339: 			name:         "hash mismatch",
  340: 			provisioning: originalProvisioning,
  341: 			apiKey: func() domain.APIKeyRecord {
  342: 				value := originalAPIKey
  343: 				value.KeyHash = strings.Repeat("0", 64)
  344: 				return value
  345: 			}(),
  346: 		},
  347: 		{
  348: 			name:         "prefix mismatch",
  349: 			provisioning: originalProvisioning,
  350: 			apiKey: func() domain.APIKeyRecord {
  351: 				value := originalAPIKey
  352: 				value.KeyPrefix = "sk_live_wrong..."
  353: 				return value
  354: 			}(),
  355: 		},
  356: 	}
  357: 
  358: 	for _, test := range tests {
  359: 		t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
  360: 			_, err := service.DecryptProvisioningMaterial(
  361: 				context.Background(),
  362: 				test.provisioning,
  363: 				test.apiKey,
  364: 			)
  365: 			if !errors.Is(err, ErrInvalidMaterial) {
  366: 				t.Fatalf(
  367: 					"error = %v, want ErrInvalidMaterial",
  368: 					err,
  369: 				)
  370: 			}
  371: 		})
  372: 	}
  373: }
